programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An environmental campaigner is suspected of starting a Bush fire to prevent developers building a new estate in the tranquil Malibu Hills - until he becomes a victim of the flames himself

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An heiress is stabbed by a stalker, but the most likely suspect is found dead - and the case is complicated when an X-ray of the victim reveals surprising results. Medical whodunit, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan is forced to step in and defend Jesse against accusations that he accidentally caused the death of a basketball player. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the case of an actress who died of an infection seemingly contracted during routine cosmetic surgery at a clinic. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A special agent forms a team of geniuses to combat hi-tech threats of the modern age, and hires former child prodigy Walter O'Brien to lead the gang. Their first mission sees them assigned by the government to help land planes at Los Angeles airport after the communications system shuts down due to a bug. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The governor's daughter is 'bio-hacked' and given a deadly virus. She only has 24 hours to live, so Scorpion is called in to save her. However, the mission causes tension with Walter after he receives a letter detailing his sister Megan's worsening multiple sclerosis. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs discovers a link between the murder of a petty officer and the break-up of his team, leading him and new director Leon Vance to uncover a case of blackmail involving an NCIS agent. The duo single out three suspects, but learn vital information has been stolen, and realise the whole Persian Gulf Fleet could be in danger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A lieutenant apparently commits suicide by jumping from an aircraft carrier and Ziva and McGee are dispatched to inform the widow - but arrive to find that she has been killed. Subsequent inquiries reveal that the injuries on her body were inflicted after death and the murder scene was carefully staged to mislead investigators. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs receives a call from his old friend Senator Patrick Kiley, who claims he is being framed for the murder of a naval commander, and seeks help to clear his name. However, incriminating evidence quickly piles up against him - and a second death complicates matters further. Meanwhile, Ziva lets her hair down to obtain fingerprints from a big oil lobbyist. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs revisits his home town to investigate an assault that left one marine dead and another badly injured, and enlists the help of his estranged father to identify the assailant. Suspicion initially falls on a local man whose daughter was dating the survivor of the attack - but later inquiries uncover a tangled tale of uncertain paternity and forbidden love. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Ralph Waite

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deeks' one-time LAPD informant Jenny Radler is kidnapped after uncovering dodgy dealings linking a lucrative car shipping outfit to a global terrorist ring. Facing a race against time, Kensi poses as a streetwise car thief to prove Jenny's findings, while the rest of the team members try to locate her before it's too late. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate the mysterious death of a Marine in Afghanistan, urged on by the victim's distraught wife, who is adamant the Navy is hiding something from her. Meanwhile, the appearance of operational psychologist Nate Getz leads to fears that an evaluation is on the horizon. Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J, Linda Hunt and Peter Cambor star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen goes undercover to infiltrate a network of Chechen terrorists, posing as a foreign fighter who wants to help them orchestrate their next attack. Chris O'Donnell, Daneila Ruah, Eric Christian Olsen and Barrett Foa star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam, Callen, Kensi and Deeks work undercover on a case involving the deaths of multiple operatives killed during a raid on a drug lord's home. They are aware that inside information is being passed, and grow determined to flush out the mole responsible. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J, Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Kensi and Deeks investigate when a radical terrorist organisation notorious for orchestrating a series of bombings in the 1970s seems to have re-emerged. When one of the group's fugitive members is found stabbed, the pair's search for answers leads them to a dangerously secluded woodland area. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah, Eric Christian Olsen and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gina's last day with the Sun Hill watch reaches a climax with a tense stand-off as manic stalker Jake Quinn holds Smithy and two schoolgirls at gunpoint, refusing to talk to anyone except the woman he has been harassing. Callum and Rachel keep cool heads and reconnoitre the scene, while the Armed Response Unit is summoned to bring a swift resolution to the situation, but hearts sink when a shot rings out around the school

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: On her first day as an Inspector, Rachel co-ordinates a joint raid with CID on a crack den, only to find the main culprits have been tipped off in advance. A frightened little girl found boarded up in the fireplace puts the team back on the scent, but Leon falls for a sob story and lets the girl's mother get away, despite her obvious involvement in the drugs operation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Rachel is frustrated when two brothers both offer conflicting versions of the stabbing of a drug dealer and Leon blots his copybook with his boss once more through his determination to help. Will gets a chance to impress Neil, who calls him in to the CID office and makes an interesting offer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A hysterical mother reports her eight-year-old daughter missing and the child's father immediately falls under suspicion - until a classmate tells the police his father was parked outside the school at the time of the disappearance. When a body is finally found there is not enough evidence to hold either suspect, but a shocking third possibility begins to emerge

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. Nurse Crane offers what support she can - but the girl's life is endangered when the young lovers go on the run. A case of petty theft brings a group of Irish travellers to the attention of the midwives, who end up treating a newborn infant and looking after an expectant mother, and it seems Fred's romance is on the up when he asks Violet to accompany him to a fundraising dance

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A water shortage means that the maternity home is busier than ever and two old schoolmates are reunited, but Marion shuns Shirley's attempts at renewing their friendship until they are brought together later in an unexpected way. Elsewhere, Barbara makes a revolutionary suggestion to help Sister Mary Cynthia treat the bedsores of her patient Tommy Mills, whose wife Gert may be more in need of care than he is. Fred thinks about taking the next step in his relationship with Violet. Drama, guest starring Una Stubbs and David Ryall

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie and Sister Mary Cynthia team up to support a deaf mother-to-be, while another patient causes great concern when a case of morning sickness proves more serious than initially suspected. On the personal front, handyman Fred's daughter is less than delighted to hear about his forthcoming wedding. Will he be forced to choose between her and Violet?

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Moth
programme.desc: Feature-length version of the drama set at the turn of the 20th century, about the fortunes of a carpenter who moves to the country to start work in his uncle's furniture business, but finds himself forced to seek employment with a beguiling woman known locally as the Moth. Jack Davenport and Justine Waddell star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A new midwife joins the team as the area is rocked by power cuts and blizzards. The new arrival is delayed by the weather and falls ill as soon as she arrives. However, she is quickly dragged from her sick bed to assist with a difficult birth. Nurse Crane and Dr Turner treat an elderly cancer patient who faces losing her house due to slum clearances, and try to convince Sergeant Woolf to allow her to die at home

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth receives a cryptic letter from a recently deceased acquaintance asking her to conduct a mysterious investigation. Instructed only to go on a coach tour of historic homes around England, she uncovers murder and intrigue. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, with Peter Tilbury, John Horsley and Helen Cherry

programme.title: The Secret Diaries of Miss Anne Lister
programme.desc: Drama, starring Maxine Peake as 19th-century Yorkshire landowner, industrialist and traveller Anne Lister, who defied the conventions of her times by living with her female lover. She kept an account of her emotions in a journal, a sizeable portion of which was written in code, and the recent deciphering of this diary offers an insight into the life of the woman who has been called Britain's first modern lesbian. With Susan Lynch, Christine Bottomley, Anna Madeley, Dean Lennox Kelly and Tina O'Brien

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' daring and controversial adaptation of Sarah Waters' novel of lesbian love set in the 1890s. The three-part drama begins as Nan Astley realises she is attracted to other women and embarks on a series of adventures, which take her from her parents' oyster parlour in Whitstable to the glamour of London. Starring Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Realising she cannot return to her family life in Whitstable, Nan attempts to earn money by posing as a male prostitute on the streets of London, disguising herself with the aid of stage costumes. It isn't long before she meets a wealthy society widow who offers her a better life - at a perilous price. Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor star

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Part three of three. Thrown out by Diana and left destitute by Blake, Nan turns to Florence for help. At first her arrival seems unwelcome, as the idealistic charity worker has settled into a life of domesticity - but the chilly reception soon turns out to be a charade and before long the pair have rekindled their friendship

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Would-be entrepreneurs Sharon and Tracey discover making a profit with a new business is not going to be as easy as they thought - thanks to Dorien, who is determined to make them sweat by keeping her hands firmly on the purse strings

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's school reunion brings back a few memories and a chance to bury the hatchet, while Dorien does some reminiscing of her own - with a toy-boy lover from her past. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Evidence reveals Mason Kemble was present at his classmate's murder and Neil arrests him. With the Press outside the station clamouring for a story, Grace struggles to get the truth out of the child and lets frustration get the better of her as he avoids giving direct answers and exhibits increasingly disturbed behaviour. Only when the youngster's father is brought in to help the interrogation is the real story revealed

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac tries to kick-start her career with a new research project, but she needs the help of Ollie, who has discovered disturbing news about his mentor. Michael returns to try to clear his name, but winning back the trust of his colleagues proves difficult when he and Ric enter a dispute over the needs of a patient. Chrissie is forced to tell Dan the truth, while Greg and Sahira's relationship takes a new turn

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: As the election approaches, Harry abandons his campaign to tend the victims of a car crash. Tess tries to persuade Alice not to leave the hospital and Selena confirms the rumours of her split with Nathan. Meanwhile, Dixie falls asleep at the wheel on the way to an accident and Cyd lectures her on the dangers of working back-to-back shifts

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will joins the CID team as a trainee DC and discovers how tough life can be out of uniform. On his first assignment he goes undercover to tackle the case of a developer who is reported missing following threats from anti-capitalist protesters. The man is found, badly beaten, at his burnt-out building site, and it soon becomes clear he had been defrauding clients to raise money to pay off a ruthless protection racketeer

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy hides upstairs after the police raid. Dot is in Rochdale. Ted can't find any work. Sarah is offered a job at the Refuge. Polly watches George's club and gets threatened

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian opens his third chip shop, but things don't go well when the fish delivery is late. Tony and Polly are spotted by Tiffany going into his flat

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Olivia is still feeling unwell and disconcerted by James's continued distance, while Elizabeth feels put out by talk at the dinner table, and Sarah tries to separate her social status from the Blighs by putting Roy in between her and the wealthy family

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tragedy mars a birthday celebration, while a late-night drink leaves James and Siegfried facing the long arm of the law. Rural drama, guest starring Dorothy Tutin, with Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: The sole occupier of a property is suspected of murder after a skeleton is discovered following a storm, but Paul digs deeper to reveal the truth. Meanwhile, Marty and new practice partner Roz find working together is not going to be easy. Drama starring Nigel Le Vaillant, Amanda Redman and Sean Maguire

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sisters Sharon and Tracey's lives are changed for ever when their husbands leave for work and never return. First episode of the popular comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: One of the yellowcoat's professionalism is questioned by a sharp-eyed passer-by who spots her entering a staff chalet late at night. With Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Gillian Taylforth

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard finds time weighing heavily on his hands, so Hyacinth decides to provide him with a hobby - a move that backfires on her in spectacular fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom reckons he can turn Babs into a musical star so Clegg and Truly offer to collect some music for him - only to discover the 'music' is actually a large console organ. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Tom Owen

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Jane and Davey investigate when the patriarch of the local aristocratic family is found stabbed to death, but find themselves revisiting an embarrassing chapter of their teenage romance. Crime drama, Starring Claire Goose

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: Jenny's ruthless boss seeks revenge against Luther, whom she believes has stolen her protegee, and demands compensation. However, the detective has other problems to deal with, namely the rescue of Ripley, who has been abducted by the serial killer in preparation for a final murderous set-piece. Crime thriller, starring Idris Elba, Aimee-Ffion Edwards and Lee Ingleby

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on a 1980s music station that resulted in the death of a controversial DJ, and looks into the subsequent suicide of the station's owner. Strickland seeks a permanent replacement for the absent Halford, but Pullman and Standing call his bluff

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: When a troubled gambler is murdered at a greyhound track it seems that his enormous debts have cost him his life. The investigation leads Ross and the team from race-fixing to sex tapes, and in the midst of it all one of their suspects - who include the victim's ex-wife and a dog handler with dark secrets - turns up dead. Detective drama, starring John Michie, Blythe Duff and Alex Norton

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Dr Hugh Knight's plans to leave Whyhope are scuppered when tragedy strikes, and Penny makes a fateful decision about her love life

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh faces chaos at home and work, with a mutiny on the farm following a drunken evening and a deadly pregnancy crisis with Hayley

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam hears his mother's voice saying goodbye, and worries that doctors in 2006 are about to turn off his life support system. When a hostage situation crops up, he becomes convinced that resolving the crisis without a single casualty will cause him to wake up in his own time. However, Gene is determined to go in with all guns blazing - putting everyone's safety at risk. John Simm stars

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Chris comes up with a surprise, while Sharon's contentment is shattered by the medical advice all women dread - she has got to lose weight

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey receives the payout from Garth's endowment policy in the post, and finds herself facing a dilemma - whether to hand the money over or take Sharon's advice and squander it on herself. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will joins the CID team as a trainee DC and discovers how tough life can be out of uniform. On his first assignment he goes undercover to tackle the case of a developer who is reported missing following threats from anti-capitalist protesters. The man is found, badly beaten, at his burnt-out building site, and it soon becomes clear he had been defrauding clients to raise money to pay off a ruthless protection racketeer

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Eddi clashes with the new senior registrar, and Oliver makes a decision about his career. Meanwhile, Jac battles to save baby Freya after she is diagnosed with a life-threatening heart condition, and Eddi is forced to put the AAU in lockdown after an unstable patient is discovered to have contracted a form of TB while in prison

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry prepares to say goodbye to the hospital and move on to his new career as an MP, but his final shift is marred by the death of a pensioner and a seemingly insincere farewell speech by Nathan. Meanwhile, Dixie bickers with new arrival Jeff, and Abs encourages Greg to pursue a relationship with Cyd

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Arun is incensed when a gang on the Bronte estate sets upon a young couple. He is then completely taken in by one of the local youngsters, who draws him into a trap that strips him of his dignity - and most of his uniform. Smithy sets out to trace the gang by way of Arun's stolen radio, and the crusading PC is shocked to discover who is masterminding the beatings and muggings - and is all the more determined to nail them this time

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tony is horrified when he is confronted over his relationship with Polly. A delighted Barry decides that it does pay to mix business with pleasure when Vanessa visits him

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca bemoans the lack of a decent birthday present from Ricky. Ted oversleeps and misses his lift to work. Anita Banks comes to look at Mark and Ruth's house

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah's annoyance at the invasion of her privacy turns to anger when she is questioned by Jack about her real name. She refuses to answer his questions and is unwilling to be drawn into a defence of her character because she feels she is the one who has had her privacy invaded. However, Jack persists in demanding an explanation, causing her to do the one thing he never expected. Australian drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James takes part in the annual village cricket match, where the already legendary Freddie Trueman is one of the captains, and Siegfried offers solace when a goldfish dies. Rural drama, with Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A gypsy boy's unexplained bruises spark an angry response from his stepfather when Dangerfield insists the lad is kept in hospital. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The staff try to stop Ted celebrating his birthday every two weeks in a bid to con gifts and free drinks out of gullible holiday-makers. Meanwhile, there may be trouble ahead when the runner-up in the Holiday Princess competition is spotted entering a staff chalet at night. Comedy, starring Paul Shane and Su Pollard

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth develops a passion for art, Rose acquires a new admirer, and Daddy has a yearning to join the Foreign Legion. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom's niece disappears following his bid to launch her into showbiz stardom at a pub. Luckily, help is close at hand as Barry, Clegg and Truly begin a desperate search resulting in a surprising reward for finding her

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective investigates when former war photographer Peter Rooker is found shot dead. It is not long before he uncovers a trail leading from tabloid newspaper baron Eddie Price and editor Melissa Booth to an executed Bosnian family, but when he enlists his wife's help in determining the psychological history behind the murder, a fatal chain of events begins

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a 1970s guitarist is investigated following the deathbed confession of a witness. Strickland, a fan of the rock star's band, reveals himself to be a keen musician, while Brian tracks down Jack and persuades him to rejoin Ucos. With guest appearances by Roger Lloyd Pack and George Costigan

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A body found hanging in the children's playground of a recently regenerated model community proves to be that of a member of the local Community Watch scheme. Suspicion falls on the chairman of the organisation Harry Wallace, a founding father who helped clean up the estate for the residents. Reid fights to clear the local hero's name, but her colleagues are not so easily impressed when it emerges he has form for accidentally killing a man. Glasgow-set detective drama, guest starring Phil McKee, with Blythe Duff

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: The opening of Hugh's new cardiac clinic is upset by life-changing news from his ex-wife, and Charlie's life is thrown into disarray when her father makes a visit

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Meryl and Carlito's relationship leaves the Knight family reeling, the tension between Hugh and Penny endangers the new cardiac clinic, and Matt reconnects with a high school crush

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: A drug dealer dies while in police custody and Sam suspects a cover-up within the unit. He orders a full investigation, though it seems none of the other officers is prepared to help. To Gene's relief, the pathologist's report appears to put everyone in the clear, but when the truth about the criminal's demise emerges, it is up to the 21st-century cop to decide whether to reveal it. programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Pilgrims Les Dennis, Lesley Joseph, Brendan Cole, Stephen K Amos, Katy Brand, Greg Rutherford, Dana and Mehreen Baig visit Bagno Vignoni - well known for its hot springs - where Greg and Brendan take the plunge in the public pool. Meanwhile, at the top of the hillside, Dana, Stephen, Les and Katy visit the Roman bath formerly used by Popes, and explore the history of St Catherine of Siena, who also used to stay here. The group members travel on to the medieval city of Viterbo, from where they will have to walk every step of the final 100km to Rome. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, where the red and blue teams explore an antique centre in the company of experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian. The auction of their gatherings is later held in nearby Chiswick

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits Salisbury Cathedral to celebrate 30 years of the girls' choir, and Laura Wright meets Christian musicians at Coventry's Nexus Institute of Creative Arts

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a mother and daughter find a country home in Norfolk with a budget of £335,000, and follows a paper trail to uncover an endangered decorative heritage craft

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of British and international TV programmes and talent, with the programme coming from the BBC's Television Centre in White City, west London. The Crown, I Hate Suzie, Gangs of London and Save Me Too are up for Best Drama Series, while other nominees include John Boyega, Josh O'Connor, Daisy Edgar-Jones, Graham Norton and Ant & Dec and the show features performances by Years & Years and Alexis Ffrench

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Prison drama, starring Sean Bean and Stephen Graham. A newly arrived prisoner has to adapt quickly to his dangerous new surroundings, while reflecting on the crime that brought him here. He finds himself sharing a cell with a troubled young man who murdered his father and is suffering panic attacks. Meanwhile, an officer encounters a threat against his son, who is serving time in a different prison

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from recent series, including Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness attempting to drive Insurance write offs around a terrifying Wall of Death. Plus, Chris reviews the Ferrari SF90 and the boys race around Alton Towers in electric cars

programme.title: Wild Weekends
programme.desc: BBC Radio 1 DJ Jeremiah Asiamah pushes three complete strangers outside of their comfort zones on a voyage of self-discovery in Torridon in the Highlands. Jeremiah's friend and outdoorsman Zeki Basan shows them how to set up camp under the stars, offers alternative cooking methods and helps them conquer an epic climb of more than 2,000ft

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A mother and daughter from Buckinghamshire answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win a life-changing sum they plan to put towards their love of animals. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore two flats in Seaham in Durham, a former bank in Westhoughton near Manchester and a building with a shop and residential space in Surbiton in Surrey. All three are sold under the hammer and their new owners explain their ambitious plans to add value to their acquisitions

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Sheriffs visit a cleaning company that owes money to a former employee, and come to the aid of a woman who took legal action against an estate agent over cleaning expenses

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction. Natasha also finds out about the value of provenance

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid and Valerie come down from yesterday, Ruhma takes Karen out, and Daniel helps a nurse who spends too much time helping everyone else. Nick Owen guest stars as himself

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Welsh Borders with a retired couple of keen gardeners who have £500,000 to plough into buying a peaceful home in the country. While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with plans for a wildlife-friendly Victorian cottage garden for two retired teachers who have bought a home in Oxford

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell scour the shops of Kent for antiques, indulging in some rocking horse riding, cat whispering and disagreements about scarf wearing on the way

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: In a bid to encourage Gary to see her in a different light, Miranda joins an evening class to learn French, but is horrified to discover it is run by her former school teacher. The hapless singleton then decides a tango class is a more romantic option, only for her plans to take another turn for the worse. Comedy, guest starring Peter Davison, with Miranda Hart and Sarah Hadland

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil apologises to Kat for not taking her mugging seriously enough and comes up with a business proposition for her. Callum is torn after Ben asks him to quit his job, Vi criticises Rainie's co-parenting set-up and Lola gives Ruby a few tips on how to handle Lily

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Manuel's pet rat escapes just as a health inspector is about to pay a fact-finding visit, sending Basil on a mad dash to track it down before disaster ensues. Madcap comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: Pilot episode for a sitcom about an undiagnosed autistic woman who is determined to have a date to take to her older sister's wedding. She throws herself into the dating scene, but finds herself struggling to cope with a world of unfamiliar social rules, where white lies, false pretences, and confusing games are considered normal. Starring Ashley Storrie

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Laura wants to know why ex Seb let their love fade away four years ago and why he no longer dates women. Will the plate questions finally help her get answers? Cain wants dinner with ex Ysabel after two years of radio silence. Will he be ready to reveal why he was constantly going MIA during their relationship? And Kush and Avi are reuniting to find out if they can be more than just friends with benefits

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The contestants face a makeover challenge this week, which requires them to come up with dazzling new looks for members of their own families. RuPaul and regular judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson are joined by the stars of comedy team Aunty Donna, Mark Samual Bonanno, Broden Kelly, and Zachary Ruane

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a pair of houses in Cheshire that share a back garden, Martel Maxwell is in a Tyne and Wear property with hardly any windows and Martin Roberts visits Rhondda

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Luke and Carl visit a classic car workshop in search of the biggest debt they have ever come across - owed to a man whose lifelong dream of owning a vintage Jaguar was shattered

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveals the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blue teams go shopping in the antique centres of Hungerford, Berkshire, joined by experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper. Eric Knowles presents and assesses their purchases

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen talks to the dead, while Sid thinks drinking is the answer. Emma and Harriet encounter two women battling with grief

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Hannah, Elayne, Rupesh and Greg vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with newlyweds who have a £625 000 budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers make over an empty plot in the Oxfordshire town of Wantage. Grace and her mother Agnes want a contemporary design with a nod toward Zimbabwe, with exotic planting and a Victoria Falls-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell go on the hunt for more elusive antique treasures. A gamble on some brown furniture may prove profitable at the Hertfordshire auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Having chased Kat's mugger, Callum blames himself when the man gets away by attacking Fitzy, and cannot open up to Ben about what happened. Kat is reluctant to take Phil up on his business offer, not wanting to get involved in anything dodgy. Ruby and Lily reach and agreement and Stuart is appalled when Vi tries to set him up with Honey

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is snappy with Ben, insisting he is fine despite being anything but - and things only get worse when Whitney and Kat ask him about the stabbing. Rainie is suspicious of Vi's sudden nice act, forcing Stuart to admit she knows about their surrogacy plans, Lola worries about Isaac when he lies about his medication, and Linda realises she will have to tell Max about the baby

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian discovers that Lucky betrayed him by calling social services, and as plans begin to move Andrei away, a desperate and powerless Kian spirals out of control. Ange and Josh are questioned about their secret relationship by Hanssen, leaving them worrying about their future at Holby, and Dom feels rejected when a patient requests Sacha to be her consultant

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea takes on a former fairground family's water can, while Suzie Fletcher tackles a miniature camel saddle - the only memento from a childhood spent in Malaysia. This curious item had been safely by owner John's side for 60 years, until an unfortunate incident with a mischievous puppy left it in tatters. Teddy bear repair duo Amanda and Julie welcome a 100-year-old RAF mascot bear called Bobby

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass in which they demonstrate a signature look in front of a panel of industry heavyweights. One contestant tackles male grooming, the second showcases special effects and the third aims to dazzle with a quick smokey eye. The next day the trio face their final creative brief, coming up with a look that demonstrates what makes them unstoppable as a make-up artist, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off. Hosted by Maya Jama

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: Lockdown is finally lifted, and in Bridgend Scott gets to open his doughnuts and coffee cafe. Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos auditions Russian cook Shev for the role of head chef, while vintage clothing entrepreneur and keyboard player Nanial prepares for his band to launch their first album

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The Psycho prequel series returns as Norma sets out in search of the missing Norman. Meanwhile, Dylan awaits news of Emma, and Romero receives a surprising proposition. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma and Norman find themselves at odds, as each suspects the other of committing horrible actions. Meanwhile, Dylan considers his future with Emma, and Romero makes a decision that will have a big impact on the Bates family. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler demonstrates how to grow herbs and flowers and use them as ingredients in a selection of dishes, including herb omelettes, bread with rosemary and salads. She also reveals how they are important to attract wildlife to gardens, something essential to the health and productivity of any allotment

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: In Stechford, Birmingham, Chris Bavin meets a group of determined volunteers entering their town into 'It's Your Neighbourhood', the entry level scheme of Bloom

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Frances Tophill introduce a special Gardeners' World from Wakehurst, Kew's wild botanic garden in West Sussex. They not only explore the gardens but also meet the gardeners, joining in on their planting projects and finding out more about the UK's largest conservation project, the Millennium Seed Bank

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales to report on plans for a major energy project harnessing the power of the tide, discovering what local people make of the plan and assess its potential impact on wildlife and fishing. Matt also checks on the health of a critical crop of carbon-capturing sea grass, and helps launch new research into mountain goats, while Joe Crowley discovers why angel sharks are being sighted off the Welsh coast more frequently

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: In a special episode, Dougie and team meet the young people who will steer the future of our countryside - the young farmers, crofters, conservationists and rural craftspeople. Anne visits a young couple who have just taken on a farm in Galloway, Dougie is in the north-west with a dynamic woman who won Young Crofter of the Year and Euan finds out how young folk can be encouraged to stay in the countryside

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole and Mairi prove that they are some of the best bargain hunters, as they create a new bargain border in Beechgrove for the price of a little work. It's an allotment special this week. and Calum Clunie is in his Leven allotment, where he is growing for upcoming shows and this week, he proves that he really knows his oversized onions

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer turns the contents of his weekly shop into seven dishes - one for each day of the week - adding new flavours to everyday recipes and creating culinary magic in just one pan. He begins by showing how to make the most of an abundance of ingredients, whether a special offer at the shops or extra produce from the garden

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Will Foster and Lynsey Elliott compete to remodel a home in Gosport, Hampshire, that holds 20 years of family memories and a lifetime of antiques and heirlooms for soon-to-be grandparents Lisa and Judy. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Live Triathlon
programme.desc: Coverage of the second leg of the Triathlon World Championship Series in Leeds. With the Olympics little more than a month away, the elite races in West Yorkshire represent one of the last chances for athletes to book their place in Tokyo - they include double Olympic champion Alistair Brownlee, who is returning from injury, as well as rising star Alex Yee. The women's race will also see a strong British team, including previous Leeds winners, Olympic bronze medallist Vicky Holland and current world champion, Georgia Taylor-Brown, both of whom have already made the British team for Tokyo. Also competing will be Scotland's Beth Potter, who recently ran the second-fastest road 5km ever for woman

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day five of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. This is the 18th overall Test match between these nations to be staged at 'the home of cricket', and if the black Caps can claim a victory it will be just the second they have recorded

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters reflect on memorable trips to the rainforest, with Chris Packham recalling a visit to Pipeline Road in Panama when he saw a staggering array of birds. Steve Backshall recounts his climb of a sheer sandstone mountain in Venezuela, while Gordon Buchanan talks about his four years living in the rainforest while learning his trade as a cameraman

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist looks at attempts to answer one of the most profound questions we can ask - are we alone in the universe? With scientists sending space probes to the furthest reaches of our solar system and beyond, the scientific search for alien life has begun and Brian explains why this quest deserves to be taken seriously, and explores the chances of extraterrestrials being found

programme.title: Arctic with Bruce Parry
programme.desc: The explorer travels to Qaanaaq, an Inuit town in the far north of Greenland, where he learns some of the skills that have enabled the native people to survive amid the harsh wilderness, including dog-sled driving and extracting water from sea ice. Hunting also remains a key part of life for the community, with supermarket prices for some staple foods set at 50 per cent more than in mainland Europe - but Bruce discovers the impact of global warming could lead to this traditional way of life dying out entirely

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin tries to help Jerome see sense and stop him taking revenge on Manboy - which would jeopardise his own, much more subtle plan for retribution

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Despite Franklin's best efforts to hold it back, the tide of change has finally come to his family, but Teddy seems to be offering a way out. Gustavo goes in search of a face from his past

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian convinces Andrei to return to hospital to have the surgery he needs, so Fletch warns Lucky to call the social services. A nervous Max needs all the support she can get as the day of her operation arrives, and Ange and Josh risk being found out when Dom and Chloe catch them sneaking around together

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross is in Portsmouth for special episode commemorating the Second World War, looking back at some of the militaria bought by teams over the years

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Philip and Lorraine in Stockport want to transform their forgotten plot into a stately garden, on a budget of £4,750. Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich Brothers to win the pitch

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalwork expert Dominic Chinea is called in to saved a beloved garden bench that is on its last legs. For owner Margaret, this is where she first met her husband in his mother's garden, and later was the seat her father took his tea breaks on. Steve Fletcher gets to work on an old calculator which is completed jammed up, Suzie Fletcher gives some much-needed TLC to an antique bridle and Tim Gunn tries to get a rusty 1930s toy scooter rolling again

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Contestants from Sussex and Northamptonshire take part in the antiques challenge, presented by Paul Martin. The pair are shown the tricks of the trade before an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing Scotland prepare starters, with returning chefs Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battling it out with newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. All three contenders take huge risks, with Ally making a ricotta that should take 24 hours to prepare, Michael attempting to impress with a vegan dish and Angela intriguing her fellow contestants with a savoury ice-cream dish

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ally McGrath creates an inventive fish dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed. Meanwhile, Angela Malik takes on the ancient skill of preparing sashimi, and defending regional winner Michael Bremner uses turbot for his meal, but which will impress the veteran chef?

programme.title: Bees, Butterflies and Blooms
programme.desc: Writer and gardener Sarah Raven tries to encourage the creation of crucial habitats for bees, butterflies and other pollinating insects. She begins in Northamptonshire by urging a village to choose wildflowers over neat grass lawns, and meets farmer Duncan Farrington, who is considering growing the plants at the edges of his crops. She also demonstrates how flowers helpful to insects can be cultivated at home

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall heads to the island of Lundy to discover why nearly half the world's population of grey seals choose to live in UK waters. Meanwhile, Lindsey Chapman helps return a stranded seal pup to the sea and wildlife cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones tracks down a group of the common seals living in a surprisingly urban setting. Plus, Matt Baker witnesses an extraordinary gathering of animals in Monterey Bay, America

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of finds from various locations, including Lulworth Castle in Dorset, Chiddingstone Castle in Kent and Wrest Park in Bedfordshire. Presenter Paul Martin also goes on a tour of the National Motor Museum at Beaulieu in Hampshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week, with the daily winner declared following a quick-fire round at the end of the show

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the West Country between the wars, setting off from the Cornish seaside resort of St Ives and ending his first in the former mining village of St Day

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson begins with her take on a classic yoghurt pot cake, which was inspired by a scribbled note she found in an old Italian kitchen. The food writer's niece and nephew are recruited to make - and enjoy - a sausage meatball supper, while a girls' night in sees her rustling up squid and prawns with marjoram and chilli. The show ends with Nigella's 'tiramisini' - a lighter, quicker version of a traditional tiramisu

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Nish Kumar, Stacey Dooley, Caitlin Moran and Robert Webb to discuss Hamnet by Maggie O'Farrell, and The Last House on Needless Street by Catriona Ward, and they also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Damion Burrows and Lizzie Fraher compete to remodel Natasha and Gael's home in Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, which has a dark and claustrophobic downstairs layout that makes social gatherings impossible. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Amanda's PTA charity fundraiser is doubling up as her birthday celebration, meaning everyone is dragged into the big event. However, the mix of sponsored cycling with Anne's lethal cocktails results in a night of high drama and big revelations. Meanwhile, Kevin's attempts to clear the air with Amanda just make everything worse, and a put-upon Anne finally loses her temper

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Famous barrister Webster is not well and not an easy man to look after. His carer Urban knows how to take the rough with the smooth, either dishing out the drugs or appealing to the old man's vanity. But on this particular night, something is coming that will test both of them to the very end. Darkly comic drama, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Aisling Bea hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up Roisin Conaty and BBC newsreader Clive Myrie joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales to report on plans for a major energy project harnessing the power of the tide, discovering what local people make of the plan and assess its potential impact on wildlife and fishing. Matt also checks on the health of a critical crop of carbon-capturing sea grass, and helps launch new research into mountain goats, while Joe Crowley discovers why angel sharks are being sighted off the Welsh coast more frequently

programme.title: The Psychedelic Drug Trial
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a ground-breaking new trial at Imperial College London, which sees a psychedelic drug tested against an antidepressant as a treatment for depression. Filmed over 16 months, the programme follows a team of scientists and psychotherapists as they compare the effects of psilocybin, the active ingredient of magic mushrooms, with an antidepressant on a small group of participants with clinical depression

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Cameras follow medical staff working in the maternity unit at the University Hospital Coventry. In addition to delivering over 5,500 babies every year, the specialised unit also pioneer tests and treatments as they undertake vital research into the causes of miscarriages and stillbirths

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell scour the shops of Kent for antiques, indulging in some rocking horse riding, cat whispering and disagreements about scarf wearing on the way

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with plans for a wildlife-friendly Victorian cottage garden for two retired teachers who have bought a home in Oxford

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives advice on plants to use for foliage with tropical proportions. Plus, Arit Anderson continues investigating the use of peat, and Carol Klein explores Oxford Botanic Garden's euphorbias

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin teaches would-be antiques dealers the tricks of the trade, pitching Pauline Oldford from Telford against Peter Gibbons from Bedfordshire, before an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Scotland chefs hope to impress with their main dishes. Angela Malik prepares a tiffin box featuring a summer biriyani, quail scotch egg and spiced quail breasts, while Michael Bremner takes a huge risk by using ox tongue as the centrepiece of his main. Ally McGrath devises an inventive take on the summer barbecue, using a new method of cooking potatoes to recreate the coals

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Angela Malik, Ally McGrath and Michael Bremner continue their battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to tomorrow's Scotland final

programme.title: Bees, Butterflies and Blooms
programme.desc: Sarah Raven encourages gardeners in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, to embrace flowers rich in pollen and nectar over traditional bedding plants, as she continues her mission to halt the decline in bee, butterfly and pollinating insect populations. She also launches a bid for a pollinator-friendly logo to be introduced at the Chelsea Flower Show, and finds out from naturalist Steve Head how her own garden can be improved

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall examines life in Australia's Red Centre, a desert landscape in the Outback, where two-metre tall kangaroos, burrowing toads and the world's most venomous snakes are among the throng of animals that live there. He also looks at how, in a place that is parched by the sun, scorched by fire and prone to unpredictable floods, creatures successfully make the heart of this island continent their home

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg heads to Countesthorpe in Leicestershire to take a look back at the Queen's Silver Jubilee celebrations of 1977. Jayne Taylor and Linda Leeke recall the day they travelled to London and ended up in the news, royal biographer Hugo Vickers recounts his role in the festivities, and former beauty queen Nicky Grossman sees footage of her younger self

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey value items and collectibles brought in by the public to Lulworth Castle in Dorset. Plus, presenter Paul Martin explores the turbulent history of the Weld family and the anti-Catholic persecution they were subject to for more than 300 years

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards test their general knowledge skills in more trivia-based games, with the winner announced on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Cornwall's county town, Truro, heading to the historic estate of Trewithen, whose gardens were stocked from China by professional plant hunters commissioned by its owner. The Atlantic Coast branch line carries Michael north to Newquay, where he discovers a pioneering surfer and braves the waves on a belly board. In the nearby village of St Mawgan, Michael is introduced to the ancient Cornish sport of 'wrassling', which surged in popularity between the wars as part of a Cornish Celtic revival

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Daniel Brocklebank and Hayley Tamaddon search for antiques in East Anglia, taking a trip to Great Yarmouth's Pleasure Beach and discovering a scheme to rehouse unwanted dogs

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring live updates from Norfolk, and Gillian Burke investigates some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams tracks down mountain hares in the Highlands, and sea slugs making a colourful appearance

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 lockdown beings to ease, the Emergency Department at University Hospital, Coventry experiences an increase in patients presenting mental health concerns. Faced with cases of self-harm and suicide, NHS staff must deal with a dwindling number of mental health beds when trying to give patients the care they need

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Jo Brand, Clive Anderson and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'new' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Managing England: The Impossible Job
programme.desc: A look at the history of the role of England football manager, featuring interviews with current boss Gareth Southgate and former incumbents Sven-Goran Eriksson, Roy Hodgson and Sam Allardyce. Past players such as Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand also contribute as the programme looks at why it is such a difficult job and how the nation's trophy drought in the sport can be ended

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part two of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first parliament of Northern Ireland. The King's visit to Belfast was the culmination of three centuries of history. This concluding film examines how political tensions and outbreaks of sectarian violence threatened to destabilise the new Northern Irish state

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan are broadcasting live from Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, as cameras capture the dramas of such birds as avocets and barn owls. Elsewhere, Iolo Williams encounters some red deer at Alladale Wilderness Reserve in Scotland, and Gillian Burke dips into the vast waters of Strangford Lough in Northern Ireland

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Turn of the Screw
programme.desc: OperaGlass Works' stage production of Britten's The Turn of the Screw, filmed on location at Wilton's Music Hall. The production was in rehearsals when the spring lockdown was declared in 2020, so the show has been reinvented as a hybrid of film-making and live theatre capture. Directed by Dominic Best, Selina Cadell and Eliza Thompson, and starring Robert Murray and Rhian Lois

programme.title: Benjamin Britten on Camera
programme.desc: Britten was one of the great classical composers of the broadcasting age and his music was regularly performed on radio and TV throughout his working life. This documentary outlines his broadcasting legacy by meeting some of the musicians he wrote for, visiting the Suffolk coast that inspired so much of his work, and hearing from the producers who brought his music to millions. With contributions by David Attenborough, Michael Crawford, Humphrey Burton and soprano April Cantelo. Narrated by James Naughtie

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the art, music and culture of Senegal, discovering a country with a cultural influence far beyond its size, with dynamic film, fashion and hip-hop scenes

programme.title: Searching for Shergar
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's most valuable racehorses, Shergar, which was famously abducted from its stables in 1983, at the height of the Troubles, and never seen again, with no ransom being paid and the kidnappers having never been brought to justice. Thirty seven years on, Alison Millar sets out to see if changed times will help her unearth the mystery once and for all - including trying to locate the place where the horse was buried, in the countryside around the village of Ballinamore

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of the planet's largest marine mammals and the secrets of their survival. Cameramen Doug Allan and Didier Noirot travel from the Indian Ocean to the Arctic seas, filming 200-ton blue whales and fighting humpbacks. The team also discovers that southern right whales possess a pair of one-ton testicles and reveals how Arctic bowheads can live to be more than 200 years old. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Documentary following Dougie Wallace as he finishes an acclaimed photography series documenting the super-rich some of London's most wealthy and exclusive postcodes. The film offers a personal and provocative portrait of one of the world's leading street photographers, as he captures life on the streets of places such as Knightsbridge and Chelsea

programme.title: Benjamin Britten on Camera
programme.desc: Britten was one of the great classical composers of the broadcasting age and his music was regularly performed on radio and TV throughout his working life. This documentary outlines his broadcasting legacy by meeting some of the musicians he wrote for, visiting the Suffolk coast that inspired so much of his work, and hearing from the producers who brought his music to millions. With contributions by David Attenborough, Michael Crawford, Humphrey Burton and soprano April Cantelo. Narrated by James Naughtie

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the deadly impact of an incendiary bomb that set fire to St Peter's Church in the historic heart of Bristol on the night of Sunday November 24, 1940. Almost 80 years later, Geoffrey Serle, who had been attending church with his father when the raid began, recounts their desperate attempt to find shelter amongst the chaos. The film also uses accounts from the time, recalling how Bristol's firefighters simply could not cope

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: The house nears completion but Griff Rhys Jones faces some important decisions and last-minute disasters as he prepares to host a celebratory party - unsure of whether the construction will be finished by the time his guests arrive

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a seascape on black canvas, complete with fierce skies, a jagged cliff and raging ocean waves

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and expert Philip Mould investigate more works of art with mysterious origins. They begin by examining a painting that owner Patrick Rice believes is by Edgar Degas. If correct, it could be worth about £500,000. However, although Patrick's father bought it from a reputable dealer in 1945, the piece failed to make the official catalogue of the artist's work - meaning it is not classed as genuine by auction houses and is currently valued at only £200. Fiona and Philip trace the artwork back through time to find out whether it really was created by one of France's greatest artists

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The search for an exciting new name in British photography continues. This week sees the six contenders challenged to capture the character of flowers in rural Herefordshire, the adrenalin of urban free running and impress Rankin with a brand advertising assignment

programme.title: Lee Miller - A Life on the Front Line
programme.desc: Lee Miller is one of the most remarkable female icons of the 20th century. A fashion model-turned-photographer, then later a war reporter, Miller chose to live life by her own rules. This documentary drama celebrates a subject who defied anyone who tried to pin her down, put her on a pedestal or pigeonhole her in any way. Often at odds with the morality of the day, she refused to be subjugated by the dominant male figures around her

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: Eamonn McCabe explores how photographers responded to historic events they witnessed in the first half of the 20th century, including the two world wars and the Great Depression

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: The documentary explores the intelligence of marine mammals, discovering just how smart whales and dolphins really are. Scientist Denise Herzing talks about the development of a two-way communication system between dolphins and humans in the Bahamas, while in Western Australia the creatures are filmed using a range of survival skills. In Mexico, grey whales seemingly empathise with humans, while the humpbacks of Alaska pool their talents to increase hunting success. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a seascape on black canvas, complete with fierce skies, a jagged cliff and raging ocean waves

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: The house nears completion but Griff Rhys Jones faces some important decisions and last-minute disasters as he prepares to host a celebratory party - unsure of whether the construction will be finished by the time his guests arrive

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and expert Philip Mould investigate more works of art with mysterious origins. They begin by examining a painting that owner Patrick Rice believes is by Edgar Degas. Fiona and Philip trace the artwork back through time to find out whether it really was created by one of France's greatest artists

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The search for an exciting new name in British photography continues. This week sees the six contenders challenged to capture the character of flowers in rural Herefordshire, the adrenalin of urban free running and impress Rankin with a brand advertising assignment

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Having restored the main farmhouse, Griff Rhys Jones embarks on the second phase of his Pembrokeshire project, turning his attention to the watermill and the derelict miller's cottage. With his son George, who is training to be an architect, overseeing the design, Griff plans to convert both these outbuildings into accommodation

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a sky hovering over snow-covered mountains and glass-like reflections

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Cabinet reshuffle rumours and possible redundancy from high office anger Jim Hacker, who considers accepting an offer to become an EEC commissioner. Comedy, starring Paul Eddington

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere neglects to invite Audrey to the annual Hunt Ball - so she plans a socially acceptable revenge. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The 1851 census revealed that Britain had become the world's first urbanised nation, an epochal change that ushered in an idealised vision of the countryside that has been celebrated by some British writers, painters and musicians ever since. Ian Hislop examines this rose-tinted view of the past in works by influential Victorian watercolourist Myles Birket Foster, whose paintings feature thatched cottages and apple-cheeked maids, and author JRR Tolkien, who portrayed the battle between the rural and the industrial in the fantasy world of The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: David Olusoga and Steven Johnson consider how, during a health crisis, the readiness of communities to engage with - and act upon - advice can have a significant impact on the spread of disease

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry is joined by a selection of BBC science experts to investigate the British public's questions about the future, trying to discover whether people could live for ever and if there will be a cure for cancer. Hannah examines the future of transport and if it will be possible to ditch fossil fuels, looks at what the weather will be like and discovers if the planet is on the verge of another mass extinction

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates this documentary exploring the vocal ranges of marine mammals. Sperm whales produce clicks to locate giant squid two miles beneath the ocean surface, and narwhals use a similar technique to find breathing holes in Arctic ice floes. Bottlenose dolphins communicate with fishermen in Brazil, humpbacks offer haunting operatic performances and boto dolphins create bat-like ultrasonic calls to help find fish in the murky Amazon River

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a sky hovering over snow-covered mountains and glass-like reflections

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As an outbreak of headlice sweeps the school, Julia finds herself accused of triggering a second wave. Ostracised by the other mums, she needs to find a way back into their good books and decides to throw a nit treatment party at her home. The gathering reveals that Anne has some big news, Meg is facing a crisis, Kevin has committed a terrible crime of passion and Liz is waiting to hear about a possible career move. Comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan
