programme.title: Would You Rather
programme.desc: Would You Rather? is a brand new series for CBBC hosted by comedian Darren Harriott. In this episode, Darren is joined by social media sensations Adam Beales and Joe Tasker, alongside his usual crew of 15 kids, as they face some big and bamboozling dilemmas to debate. Our celebrity mates Adam and Joe are tasked with trying to think just like Darren’s Crew and guess which way they vote on a series of hilarious Would You Rather? dilemmas. This episode features fun dilemmas, such as Would You Rather… Have ears the size of an elephant or a trunk instead of a nose? Have hair that changes colour depending on the temperature or have eyes that change colour depending on your mood? Time Travel into the past or time travel into the future? Tune in to hear Adam and Joe’s hilarious answers, as well as the Crew’s amusing insights.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Megan is a wildlife expert who campaigns for animal rights and has a passion for shark conservation. Her mission is to look into the threats facing sharks in British waters and around the globe, from the deadly fin trade to industrial fishing, which have caused shark population numbers to fall dramatically in recent decades. One species she is keen to know more about is the blue shark, millions of which are caught each year for their fins, which can fetch thousands of dollars in Asia and have also ended up in Chinese restaurants in the UK. Megan also investigates the story behind a recent report that one of the nation’s favourite takeaways – fish and chips – has been linked to a species of shark which is listed as globally vulnerable and is on the brink of extinction in British waters. Megan meets up with marine biologists and shark conservationists to find out more about the dangers to British sharks, and explores ways in which sharks and other fish species can be protected. As part o

programme.desc: Malaika is a wildlife film-maker and conservationist. Growing up in India inspired her to want to save the wildlife on her doorstep. She has always been fascinated by elephants and her mission is to investigate why these beautiful animals are is in such danger. The Indian elephant is classified as endangered, and Malaika learns that the biggest threats they face are forest fragmentation and living in captivity. She goes undercover to find out more about elephants in the tourist trade in Indian cities and then visits an elephant hospital run by Wildlife SOS, a charity which rehabilitates elephants rescued from captivity. Some of the elephants here were in chains for over 50 years and now have a chance to enjoy life in the wild. Malaika talks about the importance of being aware of wildlife tourism and encourages us to say no to exploitative elephant rides available on holiday.

programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how the robots astronauts use in space have helped us to create robots we use here on Earth. We discover there are lots of different types of space robots, robonauts that help astronauts to do jobs, space probes that travel to far off parts of the solar system, and landers and rovers - robots that land on planets like Mars to collect information. We learn how robots are sent on special missions to Mars and how they take pictures and send information back to Earth. Maddie visits a research centre to meet a Mars rover robot and uses her special cameras to help us see how it moves over the ground.

programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Oliver, Reau, Minnie, Theo, Francis and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. All the parents and babies have different weather-themed toys or objects too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate weather-themed toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show how they’ve been discovering the weather too. The nursery rhyme I See Sunshine features, as does a story about Baby Bear cloud spotting. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Pilgrims Les Dennis, Lesley Joseph, Brendan Cole, Stephen K Amos, Katy Brand, Greg Rutherford, Dana and Mehreen Baig visit Bagno Vignoni - well known for its hot springs - where Greg and Brendan take the plunge in the public pool. Meanwhile, at the top of the hillside, Dana, Stephen, Les and Katy visit the Roman bath formerly used by Popes, and explore the history of St Catherine of Siena, who also used to stay here. The group members travel on to the medieval city of Viterbo, from where they will have to walk every step of the final 100km to Rome. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits Salisbury Cathedral to celebrate 30 years of the girls' choir, and Laura Wright meets Christian musicians at Coventry's Nexus Institute of Creative Arts

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a mother and daughter find a country home in Norfolk with a budget of £335,000, and follows a paper trail to uncover an endangered decorative heritage craft

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: The golden eagle became extinct in Wales 400 years ago, but an escaped bird has been living in the Cambrian Mountains for 10 years. Iolo Williams scours woodlands and hills in an attempt to find this magnificent bird of prey

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway veers off the coastal path to walk along the Mawddach trail near Barmouth, taking an early-morning canoe trip and hearing a story of incredible bravery. He also visits the village of Fairbourne, and takes a ride on its special small-scale train. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones goes in search of local gold and Matt Bassett goes stargazing, but is defeated by the Welsh weather

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of British and international TV programmes and talent, with the programme coming from the BBC's Television Centre in White City, west London. The Crown, I Hate Suzie, Gangs of London and Save Me Too are up for Best Drama Series, while other nominees include John Boyega, Josh O'Connor, Daisy Edgar-Jones, Graham Norton and Ant & Dec and the show features performances by Years & Years and Alexis Ffrench

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Prison drama, starring Sean Bean and Stephen Graham. A newly arrived prisoner has to adapt quickly to his dangerous new surroundings, while reflecting on the crime that brought him here. He finds himself sharing a cell with a troubled young man who murdered his father and is suffering panic attacks. Meanwhile, an officer encounters a threat against his son, who is serving time in a different prison

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from recent series, including Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness attempting to drive Insurance write offs around a terrifying Wall of Death. Plus, Chris reviews the Ferrari SF90 and the boys race around Alton Towers in electric cars

programme.title: Hayley Goes
programme.desc: To jab or not to jab? That's the question Hayley Pearce sets out to answer as she tries to understand the fears and confusion surrounding the Covid-19 vaccine. On the way she attends an anti-mask and vaccination demo in London, does a shift at a Covid testing centre and meets frontline medical staff and scientists to separate fact from social media-driven fiction

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A mother and daughter from Buckinghamshire answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win a life-changing sum they plan to put towards their love of animals. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore two flats in Seaham in Durham, a former bank in Westhoughton near Manchester and a building with a shop and residential space in Surbiton in Surrey. All three are sold under the hammer and their new owners explain their ambitious plans to add value to their acquisitions

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: Wyre Davies reports on a boy whose body was found in a river. His mother discusses how police were initially reluctant to treat his disappearance as suspicious, and her current campaign to see the teenager who pushed him into the water for a joke charged with manslaughter

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. programme.title: Elis James: Football Nation

Starring Ashley Storrie

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is snappy with Ben, insisting he is fine despite being anything but - and things only get worse when Whitney and Kat ask him about the stabbing. programme.title: Holby City

Ange and Josh are questioned about their secret relationship by Hanssen, leaving them worrying about their future at Holby, and Dom feels rejected when a patient requests Sacha to be her consultant

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea takes on a former fairground family's water can, while Suzie Fletcher tackles a miniature camel saddle - the only memento from a childhood spent in Malaysia. This curious item had been safely by owner John's side for 60 years, until an unfortunate incident with a mischievous puppy left it in tatters. Teddy bear repair duo Amanda and Julie welcome a 100-year-old RAF mascot bear called Bobby

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass in which they demonstrate a signature look in front of a panel of industry heavyweights. One contestant tackles male grooming, the second showcases special effects and the third aims to dazzle with a quick smokey eye. The next day the trio face their final creative brief, coming up with a look that demonstrates what makes them unstoppable as a make-up artist, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off. Hosted by Maya Jama

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: Lockdown is finally lifted, and in Bridgend Scott gets to open his doughnuts and coffee cafe. Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos auditions Russian cook Shev for the role of head chef, while vintage clothing entrepreneur and keyboard player Nanial prepares for his band to launch their first album

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The Psycho prequel series returns as Norma sets out in search of the missing Norman. Meanwhile, Dylan awaits news of Emma, and Romero receives a surprising proposition. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Roisín Coll, Director of the St Andrew's Foundation for Catholic Teacher Education, University of Glasgow presents this week's Reflections. Jason Leitch, National Clinical Director of the Scottish Government talks to David Strachan about his Christian faith

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, where the red and blue teams explore an antique centre in the company of experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian. The auction of their gatherings is later held in nearby Chiswick

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits Salisbury Cathedral to celebrate 30 years of the girls' choir, and Laura Wright meets Christian musicians at Coventry's Nexus Institute of Creative Arts

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a mother and daughter find a country home in Norfolk with a budget of £335,000, and follows a paper trail to uncover an endangered decorative heritage craft

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of British and international TV programmes and talent, with the programme coming from the BBC's Television Centre in White City, west London. The Crown, I Hate Suzie, Gangs of London and Save Me Too are up for Best Drama Series, while other nominees include John Boyega, Josh O'Connor, Daisy Edgar-Jones, Graham Norton and Ant & Dec and the show features performances by Years & Years and Alexis Ffrench

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Prison drama, starring Sean Bean and Stephen Graham. A newly arrived prisoner has to adapt quickly to his dangerous new surroundings, while reflecting on the crime that brought him here. He finds himself sharing a cell with a troubled young man who murdered his father and is suffering panic attacks. Meanwhile, an officer encounters a threat against his son, who is serving time in a different prison

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Luxembourg v Scotland. Action from the friendly at Stade Josy Barthel, as Scotland play their final match before Euro 2020

programme.title: Wild Weekends
programme.desc: BBC Radio 1 DJ Jeremiah Asiamah pushes three complete strangers outside of their comfort zones on a voyage of self-discovery in Torridon in the Highlands. Jeremiah's friend and outdoorsman Zeki Basan shows them how to set up camp under the stars, offers alternative cooking methods and helps them conquer an epic climb of more than 2,000ft

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: Amy Irons and David Farrell chat to Line of Duty star Owen Teale, who is currently filming The Rig with Martin Compston. James Harkness talks to Jodie McCallum about his role in Channel 5's Anne Boleyn biopic

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from recent series, including Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness attempting to drive Insurance write offs around a terrifying Wall of Death. Plus, Chris reviews the Ferrari SF90 and the boys race around Alton Towers in electric cars

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore two flats in Seaham in Durham, a former bank in Westhoughton near Manchester and a building with a shop and residential space in Surbiton in Surrey. All three are sold under the hammer and their new owners explain their ambitious plans to add value to their acquisitions

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Sheriffs visit a cleaning company that owes money to a former employee, and come to the aid of a woman who took legal action against an estate agent over cleaning expenses

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction. Natasha also finds out about the value of provenance

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid and Valerie come down from yesterday, Ruhma takes Karen out, and Daniel helps a nurse who spends too much time helping everyone else. Nick Owen guest stars as himself

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Welsh Borders with a retired couple of keen gardeners who have £500,000 to plough into buying a peaceful home in the country. While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with plans for a wildlife-friendly Victorian cottage garden for two retired teachers who have bought a home in Oxford

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell scour the shops of Kent for antiques, indulging in some rocking horse riding, cat whispering and disagreements about scarf wearing on the way

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: The judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers visit homes in Glasgow and the Clyde Valley. A period pink property in Killearn competes with a Victorian terrace and a townhouse in Glasgow for the last place in the final of this year's competition

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil apologises to Kat for not taking her mugging seriously enough and comes up with a business proposition for her. Callum is torn after Ben asks him to quit his job, Vi criticises Rainie's co-parenting set-up and Lola gives Ruby a few tips on how to handle Lily

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Manuel's pet rat escapes just as a health inspector is about to pay a fact-finding visit, sending Basil on a mad dash to track it down before disaster ensues. Madcap comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: Pilot episode for a sitcom about an undiagnosed autistic woman who is determined to have a date to take to her older sister's wedding. She throws herself into the dating scene, but finds herself struggling to cope with a world of unfamiliar social rules, where white lies, false pretences, and confusing games are considered normal. Starring Ashley Storrie

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Laura wants to know why ex Seb let their love fade away four years ago and why he no longer dates women. Will the plate questions finally help her get answers? Cain wants dinner with ex Ysabel after two years of radio silence. Will he be ready to reveal why he was constantly going MIA during their relationship? And Kush and Avi are reuniting to find out if they can be more than just friends with benefits

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The contestants face a makeover challenge this week, which requires them to come up with dazzling new looks for members of their own families. RuPaul and regular judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson are joined by the stars of comedy team Aunty Donna, Mark Samual Bonanno, Broden Kelly, and Zachary Ruane

programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveals the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Pilgrims Les Dennis, Lesley Joseph, Brendan Cole, Stephen K Amos, Katy Brand, Greg Rutherford, Dana and Mehreen Baig visit Bagno Vignoni - well known for its hot springs - where Greg and Brendan take the plunge in the public pool. Meanwhile, at the top of the hillside, Dana, Stephen, Les and Katy visit the Roman bath formerly used by Popes, and explore the history of St Catherine of Siena, who also used to stay here. The group members travel on to the medieval city of Viterbo, from where they will have to walk every step of the final 100km to Rome. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a pair of houses in Cheshire that share a back garden, Martel Maxwell is in a Tyne and Wear property with hardly any windows and Martin Roberts visits Rhondda

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Luke and Carl visit a classic car workshop in search of the biggest debt they have ever come across - owed to a man whose lifelong dream of owning a vintage Jaguar was shattered

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveals the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blue teams go shopping in the antique centres of Hungerford, Berkshire, joined by experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper. Eric Knowles presents and assesses their purchases

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen talks to the dead, while Sid thinks drinking is the answer. Emma and Harriet encounter two women battling with grief

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Hannah, Elayne, Rupesh and Greg vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with newlyweds who have a £625 000 budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers make over an empty plot in the Oxfordshire town of Wantage. Grace and her mother Agnes want a contemporary design with a nod toward Zimbabwe, with exotic planting and a Victoria Falls-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell go on the hunt for more elusive antique treasures. A gamble on some brown furniture may prove profitable at the Hertfordshire auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Having chased Kat's mugger, Callum blames himself when the man gets away by attacking Fitzy, and cannot open up to Ben about what happened. Kat is reluctant to take Phil up on his business offer, not wanting to get involved in anything dodgy. Ruby and Lily reach and agreement and Stuart is appalled when Vi tries to set him up with Honey

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is snappy with Ben, insisting he is fine despite being anything but - and things only get worse when Whitney and Kat ask him about the stabbing. Rainie is suspicious of Vi's sudden nice act, forcing Stuart to admit she knows about their surrogacy plans, Lola worries about Isaac when he lies about his medication, and Linda realises she will have to tell Max about the baby

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian discovers that Lucky betrayed him by calling social services, and as plans begin to move Andrei away, a desperate and powerless Kian spirals out of control. Ange and Josh are questioned about their secret relationship by Hanssen, leaving them worrying about their future at Holby, and Dom feels rejected when a patient requests Sacha to be her consultant

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea takes on a former fairground family's water can, while Suzie Fletcher tackles a miniature camel saddle - the only memento from a childhood spent in Malaysia. This curious item had been safely by owner John's side for 60 years, until an unfortunate incident with a mischievous puppy left it in tatters. Teddy bear repair duo Amanda and Julie welcome a 100-year-old RAF mascot bear called Bobby

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: With next year's assembly election on a knife-edge and a sea border seen to threaten the integrity of the union, Lyndsey Telford examines how unionism can find a way forward

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass in which they demonstrate a signature look in front of a panel of industry heavyweights. One contestant tackles male grooming, the second showcases special effects and the third aims to dazzle with a quick smokey eye. The next day the trio face their final creative brief, coming up with a look that demonstrates what makes them unstoppable as a make-up artist, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off. Hosted by Maya Jama

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The Psycho prequel series returns as Norma sets out in search of the missing Norman. Meanwhile, Dylan awaits news of Emma, and Romero receives a surprising proposition. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma and Norman find themselves at odds, as each suspects the other of committing horrible actions. Meanwhile, Dylan considers his future with Emma, and Romero makes a decision that will have a big impact on the Bates family. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: A personal portrait of a South Uist crofting township, where the Greylag geese outnumber people by three to one. This film celebrates a way of life which has positively shaped this dynamic landscape and explores the challenges that the crofters face

programme.desc: Anne-Marie, Judi Love and Larry Lamb join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: For the final leg of her journey around Australian journey, Julia drives along the Great Ocean Road toward Melbourne. Along the way she watches koalas while enjoying a cuppa, discovers a golf course where kangaroos share the links, paddles with platypuses and spots wombats at dusk

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon presents the outrageous talent show as more celebrities perform behind elaborate costumes for judges Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, Robin Thicke and Ken Jeong. Here, Jamie Foxx joins the panel as guest judge and White Tiger, Turtle, Llama, Miss Monster, Kangaroo and Robot perform

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Llama, Miss Monster, White Tiger, Kangaroo and Turtle perform again for guest judge Jason Biggs and regular panellists Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, Robin Thicke and Ken Jeong. Nick Cannon presents

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Torquay for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality (2000)
programme.desc: A feminist FBI agent is sent undercover as a contestant in the Miss United States pageant when the event becomes the target of a terrorist bomb threat. As she is not exactly catwalk material, a veteran British coach is brought on board to oversee her transformation into a glamorous beauty queen. Comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Michael Caine, Benjamin Bratt, Candice Bergen and William Shatner

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Romania (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of England's final Euro 2020 warm-up game, which takes place at Riverside Stadium in Middlesbrough. With just a week to go until their European Championship campaign begins against Croatia at Wembley, Gareth Southgate gets one last chance here to assess his squad and fine-tune his tactics. Romania failed to qualify for the tournament this time around, although their national stadium in Bucharest is hosting three group-stage matches along with one last-16 fixture. With analysis from Ian Wright, Roy Keane and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: It's five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mother's past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Romania and Luxembourg v Scotland. Mark Pougatch introduces action from the Euro 2020 warm-up friendlies, which took place at Riverside Stadium and Stade Josy Barthel. Plus, highlights of Austria v Slovakia, Netherlands v Georgia and Belgium v Croatia. Ian Wright and Lee Dixon provide expert analysis

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp takes his team of England legends across Europe to tackle the continent's soccer superpower Germany on their home soil. Joining Harry again as assistant manager is John Barnes, with the team including David Seaman, Paul Merson, Matt Le Tissier, Rob Lee, Ray Parlour, Lee Sharpe, Mark Chamberlain, Mark Wright, Razor Ruddock and Lee Hendrie

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: Olympic Boxing gold medallist and Strictly star Nicola Adams begins her adventure with Bear Grylls in a quarry in the heart of Dartmoor National Park. Her first challenge is to abseil down a 150-foot sheer cliff. Later, she needs to quickly master a technique where you use upper body strength to pull yourself up a rope

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nicola was unaware it was Jimmy's plea hearing today. Kim's suspicions return, and Jamie is hurt

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel breaks the news to Peter that unfortunately his liver isn't compatible, and Johnny decides he is putting the pub up for sale. When Tyrone realises he's double booked himself with work and having the girls, Alina offers to help

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Actress and Julie Hesmondhalgh joins Alan at Manor Farm to talk about her BBC One drama The Pact, while carpenter Wayne Perrey creates his very own garden pizzeria. Plus, a report on a couple of show-stopping chickens

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Explaining that the hospital has found a liver match for Peter, Carla says an emotional goodbye as he readies himself for the op. Fiz is thrilled with the offer of flexible hours at Underworld, until Sarah reveals it was Alina's idea

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about Lanarkshire detective William Muncie's quest to bring serial killer Peter Manuel to justice. The story begins in 1946 when Muncie arrests a 16-year-old Manuel for a number of sexual assaults and nine years later Manuel is released from prison, only to commit more crimes on random victims and taunt Muncie from a distance. Douglas Henshall and Martin Compston star

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Former boxer Chris Eubank talks about his private life and career, touching on his teenage years as a shoplifter, his divorce, bankruptcy and his victory in 1990 over fierce rival Nigel Benn. Chris also recalls the 1991 fight in which his opponent Michael Watson received a near-fatal brain injury, and discusses the success of his son, Chris Eubank Jr

programme.title: Wales in the Euros - The History Boys
programme.desc: A look back at Wales' historic success in the Euro 2016 tournament, featuring the most memorable moments on and off the field with players and fans. Chris Coleman's men exceeded all expectations, reaching the semi-finals of the tournament before being defeated by eventual champions Portugal. They finished top of Group B - despite losing 2-1 to England - following wins over Slovakia and Russia, and got past Northern Ireland in the Round of 16. In the quarter-finals, they beat strong favourites Belgium 3-1, but could not overcome Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal side in the last four

programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as married couple Lalaine and Steven, siblings Farah and Ibby, and flatmates Tom and Tom use their powers of deduction to try to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mack is propositioned. Jamie tries to convince Kim to have a break. Andrea invites Charles away with her and Millie

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley begins his culinary exploration in Jordan's capital city of Amman at a bustling fruit and veg market to buy produce for his first recipe, chargrilled sumac lamb cutlets with a herby tabbouleh, which he makes in his stunning outdoor kitchen overlooking the city

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Muncie is convinced Manuel has now committed four murders, but William Watt is arrested on suspicion of killing his wife, daughter and sister-in-law, despite having been on a fishing trip at the time. With no arrest made following Anne Kneiland's murder, Muncie is determined to get the truth and visits Manuel's father, who has given his son the alibi he needed to be free of police suspicion. While Muncie finally casts enough suspicion on Watt's arrest to have him released, it is not long before another young girl goes missing

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Keane & Vieira - Best of Enemies
programme.desc: The on-field rivalry between midfielders Roy Keane and Patrick Vieira during their time at Manchester United and Arsenal respectively, when they strived for dominance in a golden period for the clubs. There was no love lost between the two combative players, who captained their teams and enjoyed many memorable battles in the quest for Premier League glory. In this programme, Gabriel Clarke interviews both men

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams narrates another compilation of TV cock-ups, all rescued from the cutting-room floor to delight the nation. Holly Willoughby reveals too much to Russell Crowe, Rylan Clark-Neal comes unstuck on the set of Supermarket Sweep, Kourtney Kardashian causes trouble on Australian news, and James Martin finds live TV is a recipe for disaster

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four more contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Nick, Clare, James and Sandra must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Actress and Julie Hesmondhalgh joins Alan at Manor Farm to talk about her BBC One drama The Pact, while carpenter Wayne Perrey creates his very own garden pizzeria. Plus, a report on a couple of show-stopping chickens

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Explaining that the hospital has found a liver match for Peter, Carla says an emotional goodbye as he readies himself for the op. Fiz is thrilled with the offer of flexible hours at Underworld, until Sarah reveals it was Alina's idea

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about Lanarkshire detective William Muncie's quest to bring serial killer Peter Manuel to justice. The story begins in 1946 when Muncie arrests a 16-year-old Manuel for a number of sexual assaults and nine years later Manuel is released from prison, only to commit more crimes on random victims and taunt Muncie from a distance. Douglas Henshall and Martin Compston star

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Former boxer Chris Eubank talks about his private life and career, touching on his teenage years as a shoplifter, his divorce, bankruptcy and his victory in 1990 over fierce rival Nigel Benn. Chris also recalls the 1991 fight in which his opponent Michael Watson received a near-fatal brain injury, and discusses the success of his son, Chris Eubank Jr

programme.title: Wales in the Euros - The History Boys
programme.desc: A look back at Wales' historic success in the Euro 2016 tournament, featuring the most memorable moments on and off the field with players and fans. Chris Coleman's men exceeded all expectations, reaching the semi-finals of the tournament before being defeated by eventual champions Portugal. They finished top of Group B - despite losing 2-1 to England - following wins over Slovakia and Russia, and got past Northern Ireland in the Round of 16. In the quarter-finals, they beat strong favourites Belgium 3-1, but could not overcome Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal side in the last four

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Muncie is convinced Manuel has now committed four murders, but William Watt is arrested on suspicion of killing his wife, daughter and sister-in-law, despite having been on a fishing trip at the time. With no arrest made following Anne Kneiland's murder, Muncie is determined to get the truth and visits Manuel's father, who has given his son the alibi he needed to be free of police suspicion. While Muncie finally casts enough suspicion on Watt's arrest to have him released, it is not long before another young girl goes missing

programme.title: Keane & Vieira - Best of Enemies
programme.desc: The on-field rivalry between midfielders Roy Keane and Patrick Vieira during their time at Manchester United and Arsenal respectively, when they strived for dominance in a golden period for the clubs. There was no love lost between the two combative players, who captained their teams and enjoyed many memorable battles in the quest for Premier League glory. In this programme, Gabriel Clarke interviews both men

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A fisherman's body is found tangled up in a North Sea trawler's net. Vera and her team investigate with the help of the local harbourmaster, and find the dead man's sons pointing the finger at the owner of a fish market, with whom they have a long-running feud. Retracing the dead man's steps, Vera discovers when he drowned and tracks down a waitress who may have been one of the last people to see him alive. However, the case becomes more complicated when a witness runs away and a boat belonging to the dead man's sister-in-law goes up in flames

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of a young man's body in an area notorious for illicit teenage gatherings, the corpse wrapped in plastic sheeting and having received a blow to the head. Vera follows the trail to a funfair where the victim used to work and a church where he sought refuge, having recently been made homeless. She delves into his family history to discover why he was on the streets when his parents lived nearby, while forensic evidence implicates the son of the vicar, who has a criminal past

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when the body of a wildlife ranger is found on a remote island off the coast of Northumberland. Her death is at first assumed to be an accident, since she was alone on the island when she died, but marks on the body seem to point to murder. As Vera delves into the dead woman's personal life, she uncovers dark secrets and old resentments

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Kim spins her web and manipulates the situation. Leyla and Liam's wedding preparations are picking up. Leanna drops some hints to Billy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A fisherman's body is found tangled up in a North Sea trawler's net. Vera and her team investigate with the help of the local harbourmaster, and find the dead man's sons pointing the finger at the owner of a fish market, with whom they have a long-running feud. Retracing the dead man's steps, Vera discovers when he drowned and tracks down a waitress who may have been one of the last people to see him alive. However, the case becomes more complicated when a witness runs away and a boat belonging to the dead man's sister-in-law goes up in flames

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of a young man's body in an area notorious for illicit teenage gatherings, the corpse wrapped in plastic sheeting and having received a blow to the head. Vera follows the trail to a funfair where the victim used to work and a church where he sought refuge, having recently been made homeless. She delves into his family history to discover why he was on the streets when his parents lived nearby, while forensic evidence implicates the son of the vicar, who has a criminal past

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when the body of a wildlife ranger is found on a remote island off the coast of Northumberland. Her death is at first assumed to be an accident, since she was alone on the island when she died, but marks on the body seem to point to murder. As Vera delves into the dead woman's personal life, she uncovers dark secrets and old resentments

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV during the 1980s, ranging from football and boxing to wrestling and double decker racing

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: Featuring the all-new Jeep Grand Cherokee and the latest Dodge Charger

programme.title: Extreme E Highlights
programme.desc: The Ocean X-Prix. Action from the second round of the season from Lac Rose, Senegal

programme.title: World Superbike Highlights
programme.desc: Action from both races in the second round of the campaign from Estoril Circuit in Portugal

programme.title: Moto GP Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the sixth round of the season, the Gran Premio d'Italia, as the riders tried to secure crucial points at the Autodromo Internazionale del Mugello

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring fourth-round singles matches in the Grand Slam tournament. Eventual champion Iga Swiatek produced a dominant display at this stage last year to overwhelm top seed Simona Halep 6-1, 6-2, before men's champion Rafael Nadal overcame qualifier Sebastian Korda in an equally one-sided contest. Following those matches, the women's and men's number three seeds progressed in contrasting fashions against home players, with Elena Svitolina winning 6-1, 6-3 against Caroline Garcia, and Dominic Thiem taking five sets to defeat Hugo Caston. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the eighth and final stage, featuring a 147km course from La Léchère-Les-Bains to Les Gets. Gary Imlach presents, with commentary by Simon Brotherton and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cups
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a rundown of the best 50 goals from the FIFA World Cup, featuring memorable strikes from Michael Owen, Dennis Bergkamp, Diego Maradona and Pele among the contenders for the most unforgettable World Cup goal in the history of the tournament

programme.title: Get Shirty
programme.desc: Documentary examining how a small underwear company from Leicester turned the world of football upside down when it invented a replica kit and established an industry now worth billions of pounds

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: The Yangtze - the longest river in Asia and the third-longest in the world - was once home to rare aquatic mammals and prodigious quantities of fish. Curious to know what still lurks in its murky currents, Jeremy explores Shanghai, the huge Three Gorges Dam and the wild uplands of Yunnan. The people he meets make him question his own preconceptions as a biologist and naturalist

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team come to the aid of a party of holidaymakers on an Austrian mountain _ who are unaware they are being held hostage

programme.title: ITV4 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood takes a look back at the glory days of British Wrestling, with some classic action from the archives.

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces more clips and profiles from the ITV sport archives, including a look at boxing star Nicola Adams, the story of rugby resilience in the Japanese city of Kamaishi, and golfer Seve Ballesteros playing for the cameras in the company of Jimmy Greaves

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Liverpool

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the ninth day's play at Roland-Garros, where the fourth-round singles matches are scheduled to conclude in the Grand Slam tournament. World number one Novak Djokovic made short work of 15th seed Karen Khachanov at this stage last year, prevailing 6-4, 6-3, 6-3. In the women's competition, eventual runner-up Sofia Kenin recovered from an early setback against Fiona Ferro to win 2-6, 6-2, 6-1, and Petra Kvitova comfortably overcame Zhang Shuai 6-2, 6-4. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Nottingham Forest
programme.desc: Between 1977 and 1984 English football teams dominated the European Cup and Nottingham Forest, under charismatic manager Brian Clough, lifted the famous trophy twice. Gabriel Clarke looks at how Clough and his assistant Peter Taylor built a championship-winning team and then shocked the world of football by dominating its premier club competition two years in a row. Featuring highlights of both of those finals and interviews with some of the key individuals involved

programme.title: When English Football Ruled Europe
programme.desc: A look at English clubs' dominance of European football between 1977 and 1984. The first of two programmes looks at Liverpool's back-to-back European Cup triumphs in 1977 and 1978, and Nottingham Forest emulating the feat the following two years

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces action from the semi-final at Wembley Stadium, which was decided by a penalty shootout. Bob Wilson presented the coverage, with analysis from Glenn Hoddle, Jack Charlton and John Barnes, and commentary by Brian Moore and Kevin Keegan

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the men's and women's quarter-finals on the 10th day of the Grand Slam, held at Roland-Garros. At this stage last year, qualifier Nadia Podoroska continued her fine run with an emphatic 6-2, 6-4 victory over third seed Elina Svitolina, and Dominic Thiem's bid to reach a third successive French Open final came to an end when he lost to Diego Schwartzman in five sets. After those contests, Iga Swiatek and Rafael Nadal displayed the form that made them champions, recording straight-set victories over Martina Trevisan and Jannik Sinner respectively. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring fourth-round singles matches in the Grand Slam tournament. Eventual champion Iga Swiatek produced a dominant display at this stage last year to overwhelm top seed Simona Halep 6-1, 6-2, before men's champion Rafael Nadal overcame qualifier Sebastian Korda in an equally one-sided contest. Following those matches, the women's and men's number three seeds progressed in contrasting fashions against home players, with Elena Svitolina winning 6-1, 6-3 against Caroline Garcia, and Dominic Thiem taking five sets to defeat Hugo Caston. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cups
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a rundown of the best 50 goals from the FIFA World Cup, featuring memorable strikes from Michael Owen, Dennis Bergkamp, Diego Maradona and Pele among the contenders for the most unforgettable World Cup goal in the history of the tournament

programme.title: Get Shirty
programme.desc: Documentary examining how a small underwear company from Leicester turned the world of football upside down when it invented a replica kit and established an industry now worth billions of pounds

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: The Yangtze - the longest river in Asia and the third-longest in the world - was once home to rare aquatic mammals and prodigious quantities of fish. Curious to know what still lurks in its murky currents, Jeremy explores Shanghai, the huge Three Gorges Dam and the wild uplands of Yunnan. The people he meets make him question his own preconceptions as a biologist and naturalist

programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets energy conservationist Andrew, who has decided to build a radical, self-heating home for his family - the first of its kind in the UK. He aims to heat the house all year round by storing the warmth of the summer sun into insulated earth banks over and around the house, and then drawing the warmth back into the living spaces in winter. However, this pioneering construction seems an exercise in trial and error from the start, and what began as a seemingly simple, pure concept becomes a complicated feat of engineering

On a Saturday night in Sheffield, police respond when a man reports that he's being attacked by his partner after she found him with a prostitute. A woman is under siege from a neighbour who claims he is sorting out neighbourhood problems by smashing cars with a hammer. And on the outskirts of Doncaster, there's a brawl outside a supermarket as three men attack someone they suspect of causing their family harm

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Another romp through the Naked Attraction archive, this time focusing on the people doing the choosing - from those that it seemed butter would not melt to the ones who revealed their darkest secrets. Highlights include the polyamorous couple picking a date to the time the bodies were covered in whipped cream, strawberries and custard

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Bear's Ben Pizza in Conway, Arkansas, a university town with a large student and faculty population. Friends Brad and Steven launched the joint in 2008 and initially it was a success, but business dropped off due to having a reputation for passionless food - most of which is microwaved

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of Anthony Trobiano, whose restaurant in Great Neck, New York, is failing even though his partner's parents have given him their life savings and he has taken out half a million dollars in loans. Gordon has some harsh criticism about the unhygienic kitchen and the quality of the food being served, but the owner has trouble accepting his advice

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The recruits go on the run across the rugged landscapes of the Inner Hebrides in a challenge designed to test their ability to evade and escape from a pursuing enemy. With limited rations and little sleep, the recruits find themselves ground down in a process where only the strongest will be able to keep going

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: Prue Leith and Dr Rupy Aujla help a couple who waste food by preparing back-up meals for their fussy children, putting their shopping bills to double the national average. They come up with a clever plan that could save £4000 a year - if the kids will eat it. Recipes include a crushed cornflake-coated chicken burger, a fresh Asian salad with the unlikely star ingredient of unwanted sandwich ham, and a repurposed leftover takeaway curry

programme.title: Pinki Elli? (2020)
programme.desc: A young working mother returns home one day to find that her baby daughter, Pinki, and the maid who is meant to be looking after her have both gone missing. She and her estranged husband set off on a frantic quest to find their child - but every time they seem to be getting close, Pinki is gone again. Thriller, starring Akshatha Pandavapura, Gunjalamma and Deepak Subramanya. In Kannada

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets Ian and Becky from Lincolnshire, who are embarking on the restoration of a rare VW Phoenix campervan. But this couple are not doing things by halves, having decided to add a sound-activated dance floor to the vehicle. He also visits Stirlingshire, where a small family business plans to convert a Sea King helicopter into luxury overnight accommodation, and travels to an ordinary residential street on the outskirts of Auckland, New Zealand, to see a dome home that is out of this world. Plus, George and Will design a front door for their rotating house

programme.title: Marmite: Love It or Loathe It?
programme.desc: Documentary examining the science behind the divisive food spread renowned for its distinctive flavour, as well as looking into its origins and its role in fighting two world wars. Superfans have their say and there is an unlikely Marmite-based recipe for cake enthusiasts, a selection of adverts, and an experiment to whether a hater can be converted into a lover

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes at Lily O'Brien's factory in Co Kildare, and follows chocolatier Chris Zammit as he takes on one of the most intricate chocolate sculptures he has ever made for the launch of a new hotel. Plus, a look at the history of the Cadbury Creme Egg, which was launched in the 1960s as an Easter special and soon a whopping 500 million of them were rolling off the production line every year

programme.title: 10 Years Younger in 10 Days
programme.desc: Cherry Healey and her team transform the look of Suzanne, who is a shadow of her former self after numerous operations including a hysterectomy and the removal of a brain tumour. They also give a makeover to Alison, who was fun-loving and confident, but caring for her elderly father, being a single mum and a tough time in lockdown has left her losing sight of who she is

programme.title: Our Cilla: The One and Only
programme.desc: The story of Cilla Black, who rose from working-class roots in Liverpool to become one of the nation's most successful and best-loved singers, presenters and all-round entertainers. Using rare and never-before-seen footage and interviews with friends and colleagues, the programme reveals just what made Cilla the woman she was, and what she was really like behind the scenes. With contributions by Alex and Sue Tatham, the first-ever Blind Date couple to get married, as well as Michael Grade, Christopher Biggins, Gloria Hunniford and Andrew Lloyd-Webber.

programme.title: The Story of
programme.desc: Profile of the talk show host, exploring his youth in Birkenhead and formative years facing a challenging career as a social worker. programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas presents a whistle-stop tour of awkward, embarrassing and cringeworthy holiday moments, as related by a selection of comedians and celebrities. Johnny's famous contributors skewer the traditional staycation, questioning the perks of rain-drenched British beaches, and whether anyone really likes going camping. Turning their attention further afield, the stars share some of their most memorable holiday tales, including transforming a hotel corridor into one long 'slip-and-slide', the suspicious discovery of a pair of chicken fillets in a swimming pool in Marbella, and a blush-inducing incident involving a naked paragliding session along a Spanish beachfront

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century artist Peter Paul Rubens, who enjoyed an active and eventful career. He was regarded as the chief exponent of the Baroque style, merging the grace of the Italian High Renaissance with the realism and landscapes of the northern tradition, and was perhaps best known for painting voluptuous female nudes within large-scale mythological allegories

programme.title: Fred and Rose West: The Search for the Victims
