programme.desc: Peter Wright is struggling to find his feet in his new practice, but when a farmer rushes in with a heavily pregnant sheep who is struggling to give birth, instinct takes over as the vet deals with the emergency. Julian Norton and his team rush a Bernese mountain puppy straight into surgery, while Matt gives Rohin a taste of exotic vetting in Cannon Hall Farm's reptile house

programme.title: Fred and Rose West: The Search for the Victims
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary examining the crimes of the couple, revealing the true horror of what took place behind closed doors at 25 Cromwell Street and other addresses in Gloucester over the course of many years. Featuring witness testimony from those closest to the victims and rarely seen archive footage. The programme also looks at recent police efforts to find the body of Mary Bastholm and tells her story, as well those of the 12 other young women known to have been murdered by Fred and Rose West

programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: A Trading Standards enforcement team raids a premises in Birmingham. Inside, they discover copious amounts of counterfeit designer goods. Meanwhile in south Wales, a team are on the hunt for a suspected dealer, and in Liverpool, a British Gas enforcement team investigates a market suspected of stealing power

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Barry, known as duo Dragtastic, get to work repairing dozens of outfits, while also dealing with their respective day jobs of cooking and teaching. Elsewhere, pensioners Ray and Christine try to adapt to the high summer heat, while ARCH horse charity founder Jill races to the rescue of a neglected white stallion

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. He begins in the cab of Puffing Billy, the world's oldest-surviving steam locomotive, which was built in 1813 and designed to move coal along a five-mile stretch of track from a Northumberland mine to the docks. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Animation for younger viewers following the adventures of the cheeky piglet, her brother George and their friends

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: More railway adventures with the lovable engine and his pals

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: Animated action adventure following a squad of six heroic puppies and their tech-savvy 10-year-old leader as they protect the community of Adventure Bay

programme.title: Monster Trucks (2016)
programme.desc: A drilling operation unleashes a subterranean creature which feeds on oil. The monster bonds with a monster truck which a local teenager is building from spare parts, and the youngster befriends the beast and helps it to return to its underground home. Fantasy adventure, starring Lucas Till and Jane Levy

programme.title: The Longest Day (1962)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama chronicling the planning and execution of the Allied landings in Normandy on June 6, 1944, from the differing perspectives of the British, German, French and American forces. Starring Richard Burton, Richard Todd, Kenneth More, Sean Connery, John Wayne, Robert Mitchum, Henry Fonda, Curt Jurgens, Roddy McDowall and Rod Steiger

programme.title: Edward Scissorhands (1990)
programme.desc: A reclusive genius creates a boy but dies before completing him, leaving his brainchild with scissor-like blades in place of hands. A kindly woman from a nearby suburb takes pity on the lonely figure and welcomes him into her home. He quickly becomes a hit in the neighbourhood - but then his new friends start to turn against him. Tim Burton's fantasy, starring Johnny Depp, Winona Ryder, Dianne Wiest and Vincent Price

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen (2003)
programme.desc: Fearless hunter Allan Quatermain teams up with a host of other characters from Victorian literature - including Captain Nemo, the Invisible Man, Tom Sawyer and Dr Jekyll - to put a stop to the nefarious Fantom's evil plot to bring about a world war. Fantasy adventure, based on Alan Moore's comic-book series, starring Sean Connery in his final screen role, Shane West, Stuart Townsend, Richard Roxburgh, Peta Wilson and Tony Curran

programme.title: X-Men Origins: Wolverine (2009)
programme.desc: Years before he joins the superhero team, mutant Logan is a member of a top secret military unit, but quits because he cannot justify his squad's brutal methods. However, when the woman he loves is murdered by his psychotic brother, Logan returns to his old enemies for help in seeking revenge - and is offered the chance to become indestructible. Superhero adventure prequel, starring Hugh Jackman and Liev Schreiber

programme.title: Victor Frankenstein (2015)
programme.desc: A sideshow performer is taken in by a scientist, who seeks his help in performing experiments to create life, and hopefully uncover the secrets of immortality. The plan goes dangerously out of control, and it falls to the devoted assistant to save his master from his dark obsessions and his monstrous creation. Gothic horror, starring James McAvoy and Daniel Radcliffe

programme.title: American Fable (2016)
programme.desc: When 11-year-old Gitty discovers that her beloved father is hiding a wealthy man in her family's silo in order to save their struggling farm, she is forced to choose between saving the man's life or protecting her family from the consequences of their actions. Thriller, starring Peyton Kennedy, Richard Schiff and Kip Pardue

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: Follows the lives of three members of St Saviour's Deaf Church: an artist, an emigration pioneer, and a lithographic printer

programme.title: The Malta Story (1953)
programme.desc: The island of Malta comes under siege during the Second World War, as both Allied and Axis forces see it as vital to their supply lines. A British reconnaissance pilot arrives on the island, and plays a crucial role in defending it from the advancing enemy forces. Drama, starring Alec Guinness, Anthony Steel, Jack Hawkins, Muriel Pavlow and Flora Robson

programme.title: Cowboy (1958)
programme.desc: A Chicago hotel clerk befriends a visiting cowboy and soon talks his way into joining him on a cattle drive so he can impress the woman he loves. The tenderfoot has no idea of what awaits him as he ventures out into the Wild West, an experience that forces him to undergo a dramatic change. Western, starring Jack Lemmon, Glenn Ford, Anna Kashfi and Brian Donlevy

programme.title: Sea of Sand (1958)
programme.desc: Allied troops fighting in North Africa in 1943 are redeployed as saboteurs behind enemy lines to blow up Rommel's desert fuel dumps. A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball

programme.title: Apache Uprising (1965)
programme.desc: Western, starring Rory Calhoun as a tough-talking cowboy with a heart of gold who bravely defends fellow stagecoach passengers from a potentially lethal Indian attack. Also starring Corinne Calvet, Lon Chaney Jnr and John Russell

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul

programme.title: The Greatest Showman (2017)
programme.desc: After losing his job as a bank clerk, PT Barnum creates and develops his circus in New York in the mid-1800s with the help of playwright Phillip Carlyle and an adopted family of entertainers shunned by society. Musical drama, starring Hugh Jackman, Zac Efron, Michelle Williams, Rebecca Ferguson and Zendaya

programme.title: Brassed Off (1996)
programme.desc: The leader of a South Yorkshire colliery band is determined to see its members compete in a prestigious competition, despite his own failing health - but the musicians are more concerned with the threatened closure of their pit. Drama, starring Pete Postlethwaite, Ewan McGregor, Stephen Tompkinson and Tara FitzGerald

programme.title: A Taxi Driver (2017)
programme.desc: A taxi driver from Seoul unintentionally becomes involved in the events of the Gwangju Uprising in 1980. Jang Hoon's drama, starring Song Kang-ho, Thomas Kretschmann and Yoo Hae-jin. In Korean, English and German

programme.title: Hell Below Zero (1954)
programme.desc: A thrill-seeking explorer joins a whaling ship and is persuaded by the female captain to investigate the disappearance of her father on an earlier expedition. The pair become suspicious of her former lover - and are proved right when he leaves them for dead in Antarctic waters. Action adventure, starring Alan Ladd, Joan Tetzel, Basil Sydney and Stanley Baker

programme.title: At Gunpoint (1955)
programme.desc: A peaceable shopkeeper becomes a local hero when he accidentally kills the leader of a gang of bank robbers. However, when the dead bandit's partners in crime come looking for retribution, he finds the friends and neighbours who praised his bravery are now about to abandon him. Western, starring Fred MacMurray, Dorothy Malone and Walter Brennan

programme.title: The Stranger (1946)
programme.desc: A war crimes investigator is on the trail of an infamous Nazi. Drama, directed by and starring Orson Welles, with Loretta Young and Edward G Robinson

programme.title: Stalag 17 (1953)
programme.desc: The inmates of a German PoW camp are convinced there is a traitor in their midst when the guards seem to be forewarned of their escape attempts. An unassuming sergeant content to remain a captive until the end of the conflict becomes the focus of suspicion as mob mentality takes hold. Billy Wilder's Second World War drama, starring an Oscar-winning William Holden, with Don Taylor, Otto Preminger and Harvey Lembeck

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio

programme.title: Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets (2017)
programme.desc: When a dark force threatens a vast metropolis, special operatives Valerian and Laureline must race to identify the menace and protect the future of the universe. Luc Besson's epic sci-fi adventure, starring Dane DeHaan, Cara Delevingne and Clive Owen

programme.title: T2 Trainspotting (2017)
programme.desc: Recovering heroin addict Mark Renton returns to his Edinburgh roots after 20 years living overseas, hoping to make peace with the friends he abandoned. However, starting anew proves far from easy, especially when old partner in crime Begbie escapes from prison, still brooding on Renton's betrayal. Danny Boyle's drama sequel, starring Ewan McGregor, Robert Carlyle, Ewen Bremner and Jonny Lee Miller

programme.title: Learning to Drive (2014)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Melissa & Joey

programme.title: Scrubs

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits one of Kenya's largest tea plantations and discovers that tea comes in multiple grades and flavours, and every batch can be subtly different. Matt Tebbutt dives in Cornwall for the edible seaweed that has started cropping up on supermarket shelves and Kate Quilton heads to Slovenia and Northern Ireland, eager to learn about the increasing use of rosemary in foodstuffs

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George Clarke checks out unusual mobile homes, including a converted railway cattle carriage and a vintage Morris Minor Caravanette in need of extensive renovation. He also begins a tour of Spain's tiny homes in the Sierra de Las Nieves mountains in southern Andalucia, and embarks on another project, as he and Will Hardie decide to transform the rusty shell of a 1970s builders' van into a luxury camper

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Architect Ed Lipton renovates a house for a couple who moved in nine years ago for £220,000, but with two young sons are fast outgrowing the property and cannot afford to move. Ed's bold ideas are held up by planning problems and once the build starts, Rachel and James agree to move out so progress can be quicker, but it has to be completed in just eight weeks. Knocking down the garage and replacing it with a dramatic, double-height extension to give the living space a real sense of drama is one of the potential solutions, although the budget of £60,000 is set to test Ed's skill to the limit

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The presenter attempts to unlock the potential of old houses, including the rules when it comes to putting a kitchen in a period home. He also meets a woman who bought a 400-year-old Devon cottage, and has some solutions for turning this old house into a new home

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Old Stables, Somerset, where Finnish-born Nina Dodd and her husband Andy welcome guests to their Scandinavian-style accommodation. While some are impressed by the immaculate interior, David and Erica Hensley are not pleased about the no-shoes policy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Rigsby's Guest House in Hertfordshire, where owner Morris Cockman entertains his rivals with a visit to a local zoo. Not all of his guests are impressed, and a slow breakfast service does not improve his chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Erica Hensley host at the Fairway in Blackpool, where they hope to win over their guests with a spot of classic seaside entertainment. After taking their fellow contestants on a horror walk, the couple will be wary of any unexpected surprises when it comes to their scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Robin Hughes is the final host of the week in the converted Dent Station in the Yorkshire Dales. He organises a butchery competition and high-end food, before some home truths about the group dynamic are revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Robin Hughes' remarks from across the week are discussed, before the winner of the competition is revealed

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

The proprietor tries to win his rivals round with his unique sense of humour, but tensions rise when the contestants go for a spin on a go-kart track

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sherrill Tacy hosts at the Sherwood Guesthouse in New Brighton on the Wirral, and is praised for the establishment's value for money. However, there is upset when her breakfast leaves a lot to be desired, and her final review is far from perfect

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Final hosts Mark and Rachael Dann welcome the contestants to the Big Bear Lodge in Shropshire. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics race against the clock as a man shows signs of a stroke, and a drunk man is stranded in the street and has to be taken to hospital for his own safety

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 63-year-old builder falls from a great height while working on a construction site, and his daughter shares her stories of the sacrifices he made for her. Doctors fear a teenager who has been hit by a car may have sustained brain injuries, while a man who collapsed while playing golf talks about his unconventional upbringing in Kenya and how he had to adapt to fulfill his dreams

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medics battle to keep 48-year-old Paul alive as he suffers a series of heart attacks. Roger's emergency stop halts his day at a scooter rally while, in Cumbria, there's a dramatic mountainside rescue for seriously injured cyclist Nick

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Amber and Colin turn their bedroom into a dream vintage haven. After an initial assessment, the presenter begins by getting rid of any mass-produced furniture

programme.desc: Newly-weds Karen and John Leonard from Essex are about to retire. With the last of the six boys they have between them about to leave home, they're looking for a new adventure and a complete change of lifestyle. Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties in the sunny south of Gran Canaria for their budget of £175,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Tiverton in the West Country to meet petrolhead Roger. Henry finds part of a tornado jet that he wants to turn into a table, while Simon finds a pair of exhaust pipes, which he thinks could become a trendy pair of lamps

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Worcestershire to meet mechanic Adrian. Simon restores an old, wrecked armchair into some punk-rock furniture

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers search for heirs on a huge family tree, and spend days trying to track them down. Plus, the investigation into a former civil servant's £160,000 estate, and details of more unclaimed properties

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A family of four hope to turn their passion for cycling into profit by opening a bicycle tour company in France and a diving instructor in Marbella buys out his only competitor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Sancton, East Yorkshire and The North Star Club, owned by Carolyn Van Outersterp, who is joined in the competition by her daughter Galatea, where they hope their peaceful woodland cabins win over their guests. On arrival, guests Luke and John are critical of the cleanliness, and the pricing of the rooms raise eyebrows. At dinner, the reveal of a self-catering breakfast ruffles some feathers, but it's the lack of wi-fi that divides the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Friends B&B in Blackpool, Lancashire run by Luke and John Conway, where a lack of attention to detail in the rooms leaves one critic unimpressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Lincoln Holiday Retreat, where guests Luke and John's great first impressions are muddied due to cleanliness concerns

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Chapel in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, whose owners Mark Hinchliffe and Heidi Donohoe, pride themselves on offering something a little different in their converted chapel. On arrival, the guests are wowed by the interior and artwork on show. However, Luke and John discover some grime underneath the glamour and Debbie is concerned about her traditional Chinese bed. At dinner, all the guests congratulate Mark and Heidi on their pretty property, but things soon turn ugly when talk turns to the looming payment day as it becomes clear that the B&B'ers have grievances to air

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The North Star Club is first and owner Carolyn Van Outersterp has some cleanliness issues she wants to brush up on after receiving a score of one earlier in the week

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A simple case becomes complicated when family secrets are uncovered, while another reveals a great-grandfather's life on the docks in the early days of diving. An uncle's part in the defeat of Nazi Germany in the Second World War is also highlighted. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be traced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Two of the young Asian elephants contract the herpes virus, leaving the keepers doing everything they can to save the animals' lives, using anti-viral medication and a pioneering blood transfusion. When silvery gibbon Tilu gives birth, dad Alven has to keep their older son, tearaway Eko, in check. The keepers wonder why jaguars Napo and Goshi have not mated after six years together, and a new species of cricket arrives at the insect house

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie prepares to end the year in style by throwing a Christmas dinner to remember. However, with the ancient range cooker out of action and the 19th-century bread oven put into service cooking the turkey, it's a tall order to deliver a five-course extravaganza for 17 guests. Angel sets Dick the task of finding and chopping the perfect logs for her Christmas table display in their 12-acre grounds. Christmas cheer threatens to run dry for south Londoners Lee and Belinda as freezing temperatures and a broken boiler put their first ever festive event in peril

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of 37-year-old Angelo Mastropietro, as he transforms a damp, dark and abandoned cave in Worcestershire. Having recently been diagnosed with multiple sclerosis, Angelo wants to create a retreat to help him cope with the news and become a 21st-century caveman. He has a budget of £100,000 and plans to almost single-handedly carve, cut and drill into the hillside to make the cave comfortable for contemporary living. However, there is no manual for a project of this kind, and the unique challenge soon becomes an obsession

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: Exploring the Scottish Highlands from the North Sea coast to Scotland's most northerly point, taking in Loch Ness, the Great Glen Fault and Ben Nevis along the way

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by BAFTA-winning actor and writer Jessica Hynes to explore the ultimate staycations in Jessica's home county of Kent. The pair visit a lovingly restored early 1900s windmill outside the village of Benenden, before travelling to the Isle of Sheppey, where they explore Elmley National Nature Reserve, the only nature reserve in England where you can stay overnight

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 54-year-old John, who was involved in a cycling accident in which he went over his handlebars and was unconscious for 10 minutes. Sydney, who's 87, has reluctantly come to A&E after he experienced severe chest pains, while Alan has fallen and badly fractured his elbow

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words and numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Victoria Coren Mitchell going up against guest captain Lee Mack and Bob Mortimer. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the vowels, consonants and numbers

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Australia to investigate eucalyptus, which is found in throat lozenges and chewing gum, with a local farmer shows her the clever way the highly toxic plant is made safe for human consumption. Jimmy Doherty is in Poland to find out about sauerkraut, before learning about a remarkable new use for its juice in a French power station, while Matt Tebbutt examines whether coffee, tomatoes, potatoes or even red wine should be kept in the fridge

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp introduces new crafts, skills and techniques for interior decoration. In this edition, she reveals how to create a personalised vintage doormat and coasters, using spray paint, old tiles and serviettes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Michelle and Gary Cole from Birmingham have both suffered from ill health over the past few years and have decided that the rejuvenating effects of the Spanish sun are just what the doctor ordered. They're looking for a holiday home where they and their two kids can spend quality time together. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties on the Costa Blanca for their £65,000 budget

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.desc: A company tries to find beneficiaries to the £400,000 estate of a woman from Enfield, before hearing news that threatens the whole investigation. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs are still to be found

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Special edition of the documentary which gets up close and personal with the animals in Chester Zoo's aquatic habitat. Rare blind cave fish Tiny Tim needs a love potion to help him become a father, and Popeye the chalk bass - the toughest fighter on the reef - needs an urgent operation after injuring his eye in an altercation. The zookeepers hope to breed from Ophelia the Mexican lake salamander, of which there are only 100 females left in the world, while young penguin Frazzle needs to find a mate and settle down to family life. But he cannot seem to stop flirting with every female in sight

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: A recent storm leaves Lee and Belinda's castle keep in need of urgent repairs - and Lee must face his fear of heights if he is to repair the hole in the roof

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of teacher Michele Long and architect Michael Howe, who want to restore and transform a 100-year-old blacksmiths in Co Antrim. With virtually no practical experience, Michael wants to do as much of the project as possible by himself. However, the weather on the coastline is fierce, and the freezing weather coupled with a tight budget of 150k means the build is stretched from one to three years

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend take on a 1924 Austin 7 Chummy - former painter and decorator Christopher's first car that he bought in the 1960s

programme.title: Car S.O.S. Special: 7 Day Challenge
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part special filmed at the British Motor Museum, in which Tim Shaw, Fuzz Townshend and guest Ross Kemp try to restore a Series 2 Land Rover in a week

programme.title: Car S.O.S. Special: 7 Day Challenge
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tim and Fuzz race to finish 80-year-old Brian's 1959 Land Rover, while Rick Wakeman drops by to check out their progress and take a spin in his dream car

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Neil, 53, is rushed into hospital with severe chest and lung injuries after coming off his bike, and 40-year-old Michael is brought in with chest pain and a heart rate of up to 180bpm after collapsing on a golf course. Lastly, doctors treat 27-year-old George, who accidentally grazed his eye on a low hanging branch

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words and numbers quiz. programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend take on a 1924 Austin 7 Chummy - former painter and decorator Christopher's first car that he bought in the 1960s

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt visits the town of Tequila on the trail of Mexico's national drink as he tries to find out where the idea of a worm in the bottle came from, and Jimmy Doherty learns how Poles transform carp into a much-loved national delicacy. Kate Quilton visits Spain to explore the difference between tangerines, mandarins, satsumas and clementines

programme.desc: Lucas and Adam visit a huge collection of military items and vehicles just outside of Melbourne

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef's travels take him to the River Tarn, where he joins a local angler to fish for pike. He also rustles up a popular confit of duck with braised red cabbage, and meets a chef in Moissac whose dishes are a celebration of seasonal specialities

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food continues his barge journey through south-west France, sampling the famous sausages as he reaches Toulouse and creating the country's most popular ready-meal cassoulet further downstream

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank visit South Carolina, where they find a collection of brass instruments, an art deco fan, and a hoard of rare collectibles

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank make a gamble by buying an expensive samurai outfit, and unearth a collection of rare motorcycles

programme.title: Fast Justice
programme.desc: The Sentinels floor it in a dramatic high-speed pursuit after a suspected drug dealer. Plus, a lucky break, as a car that previously gave them the slip drives right past the cops

programme.title: Secrets of the Supercars
programme.desc: The Tesla Model S is put under the spotlight and car customiser Elo is in Florida tracking down the Sebring Vanguard Citicar

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head to Norway to find out how a selection of cars fare when taking part in Winter Olympic events. Their experiments include firing a rocket-powered Mini off a ski jump, tackling the biathlon with 4x4s, staging a game of motorised ice hockey and timing the Jaguar XK in a speed-skating race

programme.desc: Michael Portillo spends the night in a converted post office railway carriage before continuing his journey from Swindon to Penzance, heading for Par, where he meets miner Ivor Bowditch to explore the largest clay mine in the world. In Mevagissey he discovers how the methods for pilchard fishing have changed over the years, and on a visit to the Lost Gardens of Heligan, Michael learns about the 1,000-acre estate's history

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo undertakes the last leg of his journey from Swindon to Penzance. He travels to Perran Sands, where he searches for the lost church of St Piran, a place believed to be one of the earliest Christian sites in England. In Redruth, he explores the remains of the Cornish tin industry, before heading to St Ives, where he spends the night in Treganna Castle. The former politician finishes his trip in Penzance, discovering how railways link remote communities to the rest of the country

programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of the Olde Stone Mill restaurant in Tuckahoe, New York, which is struggling to attract customers, leading to debts of nearly $1million. Gordon feels the owner is more interested in his own appearance than how his food tastes and a screaming match ensues

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to family owned Italian restaurant Giuseppi's in Macomb Township, Michigan. Father and son Joe and Sam Borgia's constant fighting is driving customers away, and Gordon discovers one of the restaurateurs is concealing a serious health problem. Nevertheless, he sets about rescuing them from disaster and turning around the failing business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Rhona Brennan and daughter Nina find the perfect family holiday home that could also work as a B&B in the French region of Limousin. Working within a budget of £165,000, Jasmine delivers five properties in spectacular locations, but it remains to be seen whether the pair will be bowled over by one of the options

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Having recently sold their business in Edinburgh, Gary and Kim Harrison cannot wait to find their dream holiday home on their favourite Caribbean island of St Lucia to enjoy their retirement. With a budget of £300,000, Danni Menzies delivers five properties with stunning views, but which one - if any - will steal their hearts and prompt them to make an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps dad and daughter Stephen and Stephanie, who are moving to the French region of Poitou-Charentes with Stephanie's young son Carter. They are hoping to set up their own glamping business, and want a character property with outside space on a budget of £250,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps a couple from the Wirral find a new home in the Costa Blanca that is big enough for lots of relatives on both sides of the family to regularly visit

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating places to live, work and play. In the second episode, George meets a mother determined to turn a broken shed into a three-room haven for her family, and a young couple trying to create a luxury home on a houseboat. Elsewhere in Chile, George visits a stunning temple, before returning home to continue building work on his observatory

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets energy conservationist Andrew, who has decided to build a radical, self-heating home for his family - the first of its kind in the UK. He aims to heat the house all year round by storing the warmth of the summer sun into insulated earth banks over and around the house, and then drawing the warmth back into the living spaces in winter. However, this pioneering construction seems an exercise in trial and error from the start, and what began as a seemingly simple, pure concept becomes a complicated feat of engineering

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits a gastropub in Oak Park, California, pitched close to ranches often used in TV and film productions. Kirin and Angelika Stone own the place, but have lost their home in the wildfires that ravaged California. Can Gordon and his team help save their restaurant?

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The recruits go on the run across the rugged landscapes of the Inner Hebrides in a challenge designed to test their ability to evade and escape from a pursuing enemy. With limited rations and little sleep, the recruits find themselves ground down in a process where only the strongest will be able to keep going

programme.title: 21 Day Body Turnaround with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The journalist and doctor asks five volunteers to put their lives in his hands as he attempts to turn their health around in just 21 days. Initial testing reveals all of them to likely be at risk from debilitating conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, heart disease, chronic inflammation and stress. Most have the aerobic fitness age of someone at least 20 years older. In the first episode, Michael investigates their digestive health and starts them out on a new training plan in conjunction with GP and fitness trainer Dr Folusha Oluwajana

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Catching up with two sets of house-hunters with different requirements. In 2010, Carole and Alan, and their daughter Lauren, were casting a wide net over Somerset and Devon. They needed a rural spot to accommodate Lauren's dog training business, as well as a home with the potential to do holiday lets to keep Carole and Alan busy. And in 2019, Ros and Hannah wanted to buy their first home together, but finding somewhere that ticked all of their boxes proved. Now they programme returns to see how both couples are getting on

programme.title: Britain's Most Luxurious Hotels
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Corinthia, one of London's most opulent hotels. A royal visitor leaves the staff with the very important duty of looking after her dog, while front of house director Jochem has his eyes on a new job as the hotel manager, but has to impress the managing director with his vision for the hotel

programme.title: The Scottish Island that Won the Lottery
programme.desc: The story of North Uist, a remote island in the Outer Hebrides with a population of 1500 people, who collectively won a £3million windfall and are now faced with deciding how to spend it. The programme follows fisherman Donald, crofter Attar, postal worker Pamela, peat cutter Duncan, and whisky distillery entrepreneurs Kate and Jonny, as they face a very different future

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Culinary contender Natasha Worral hopes her daughter's favourite dish of Greek meatballs will stump chef Sophie Michell to get some money in her prize pot. Natasha also has to beat Sophie at her own family favourite of truffle mac 'n' cheese, as she cooks herself closer to that enticing jackpot sum. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at My Way Guest House in Broompark, Co Durham, where Kaye Horner and her daughter Lily are counting on their cleanliness to gain top marks

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three Essex couples compete for the cash prize of £1,000 with Kellie and Scott, Stuart and Jamie, and Nick and Lisa taking turns to try to make the best impression. Married couple Kellie and Scott kick-off with a love-themed menu, but decorating company owner Stuart and his lady-of-leisure wife Jamie are shocked to see that the menu consists of not one, but two nearly identical dishes to their own menu

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps sisters Annabel and Victoria, who have a budget of £100,000, to find an accessible apartment on the Costa del Sol, where their parents once owned a holiday home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Adrian Simpson meets Colin and Veronica Moore, who want a holiday home in St Lucia where they can escape the British winters, so he searches the Caribbean island for properties on a budget of £200,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring glamour model Annierose Crone hosts the first dinner party in Coventry, and prepares a menu featuring sausages in the starter, main course and dessert. Her guests are cleaner and wrestler Joanne Edwards, pub manager Tony Jackson, chocolatier Chris Tabor and salon owner Michelle Russell

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Master chocolatier Chris Tabor hosts the second dinner party in Coventry, but his elaborate menu provokes some mixed reactions from the others, and he ends up falling out with pub landlord Tony Jackson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time pro-wrestler Joanne Edwards hosts the third party in Coventry. Her risky strategy is to prepare dishes she has never made before, and the lack of knowledge shows when she manages to injure herself twice while cooking the meal

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Publican Tony Jackson hosts the fourth dinner party in Coventry. His guests have high expectations for his pub-themed event and while Michelle Russell thinks he may be the man to beat, Chris Tabor tries to spoil the evening by criticising every aspect of the meal

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hair salon owner Michelle Russell hosts the final dinner party in Coventry, and the evening begins with champagne in the garden, but the proceedings take a turn for the worse when Tony Jackson decides to irritate the other guests and Joanne Edwards makes a shocking announcement. The winner of the £1,000 prize is also announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of the Olde Stone Mill restaurant in Tuckahoe, New York, which is struggling to attract customers, leading to debts of nearly $1million. Gordon feels the owner is more interested in his own appearance than how his food tastes and a screaming match ensues

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Anna and Philipp continue their mammoth restoration project, as they battle to complete the family apartment in their chateau's attic before opening as a B&B in the spring

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows five properties to Pat and David, who have been married for 44 years and are selling up in Northampton to move to Portugal's Silver Coast

programme.title: 21 Day Body Turnaround with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Michael gathers the five volunteers together for a rooftop high-intensity training session with Dr Folusha Oluwajana. Meanwhile, a self-confessed 'junk food vegan' makes changes, and the group go cold-water swimming at a balmy eight degrees centigrade

programme.title: Britain's Most Luxurious Hotels
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Corinthia, one of London's most opulent hotels. programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Famous faces, including Denise Van Outen, Nick Grimshaw, Mo Gilligan, Rylan Clark-Neal, Gyles Brandreth, Shaun Ryder, and Martin and Roman Kemp, turn their hand to being the country's most opinionated viewers, sharing their thoughts on what they have been watching during the week

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows. From May 2020

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A look back at the times that the unique dating show has broken new ground, featuring stories from across the spectrum of gender and sexuality. There is also a catch-up with Lauren Harries, celebrated transsexual woman and the first celebrity to appear in a pod, and Josh, who was on the third series, to find out if he is still a virgin

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An on-call stroke doctor has a busy shift in the resus department, and shares his personal experiences of working on the front line during the first week of the pandemic. When a 19-year-old is treated for injuries sustained in a motorbike crash, his mother reflects on leaving her children in Colombia in search of a better life for them abroad. Other patients include a builder with an ankle injury sustained playing basketball, and a 72-year-old with a suspected stroke

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 69-year-old man is rushed to St George's after collapsing on a tennis court, and the resus team has to work quickly to get his dangerously high heart rate under control. Other patients include a father suffering from shortness of breath and a young man who sustained a head injury playing football. Newly qualified consultant Mehrad recounts what it's like to work under pressure and the satisfaction of saving a patient's life

programme.title: The Scottish Island that Won the Lottery
programme.desc: The story of North Uist, a remote island in the Outer Hebrides with a population of 1500 people, who collectively won a £3million windfall and are now faced with deciding how to spend it. The programme follows fisherman Donald, crofter Attar, postal worker Pamela, peat cutter Duncan, and whisky distillery entrepreneurs Kate and Jonny, as they face a very different future

programme.title: Football's Gambling Addiction
programme.desc: Former Scottish Conservative leader Ruth Davidson reports on the dangerous relationship between football and gambling. She examines how the sport has become increasingly reliant on the additional profits that the gambling industry can generate, and questions what dangers this poses to younger fans and why the Government has taken so long to address this issue

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: In the city that never sleeps, the documentary discovers a homelessness epidemic in New York's shelter system, made worse by the Covid crisis. Reporter Krishnan Guru-Murthy witnesses the harsh reality of addiction, fear, violence and intimidation in a system meant to be helping the city's most vulnerable men, women and children. With one epidemic feeding another, there is now more homelessness in New York than at any time since the Great Depression, and it's disproportionately affecting black and brown people

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Old Orleton Inn in Wellington, Shropshire, where an award-winning breakfast is on offer - but a lack of showers does not go down well

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, married couple Victoria and Andrew kick off with an Italian-themed menu. This divides girlfriends Ciara and Carley, while Terri and husband David aren't keen on the couples' decision to invite a gospel choir to perform. Day two sees fitness-loving Ciara and Carley cooking up a healthy, clean-eating menu. However, Victoria and Andrew are left feeling worse for wear after a game of smoothie pong involving sprout juice, and Terri is less than pleased with the sportswear dress code. Finally, it's retro-loving Terri and David's chance to bring back the '70s to try to bag the grand

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman meets Kent couple Alison and Lee, who are making a permanent move with their four dogs to the countryside of Albox in Almería, and have a £150,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps find a suitable property for Mark and Wendy Lang, a couple from Liverpool with a £90,000 budget who want to start a new life on Spain's Costa Almeria

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Presenter Sara Damergi helps Chris Grandison and his grandmother Patricia Blythe search for a holiday home in Malta. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Healthcare worker Bev Stewart hosts the first round of the dinner party challenge in Keighley, West Yorkshire, opting for a schooldays-themed evening featuring fish fingers, meat and potato pie, and jelly and ice-cream. However, her choice fails to impress hair salon owner Rebecca, computer network engineer Adam, sales adviser Philomena and business owner Charlie

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Computer engineer Adam Bolam-Peel hosts the second party in Keighley, West Yorkshire, and plans plenty of surprises - including Northumbrian pipe playing and a poetry session. However, picky guest Philomena finds fault with his cluttered house, while Charlie cannot resist goading Rebecca about her teeth

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Image-conscious salon owner Rebecca Saunders knows she is not a great cook, and hopes her entertainment will make up for that as she hosts the competition in Keighley, West Yorkshire. She gives mock makeovers to the guests, and they are all surprised by Adam's transformation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sales adviser Philomena Golenya hosts the penultimate dinner party in Keighley, West Yorkshire, and tries to impress her guests by writing her four-course menu in Italian. However, a broken chair and a small fire in the kitchen threaten to derail proceedings, until her Italian dancing proves to be a hit with one of the guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business owner Charlie Walker hosts the final dinner party in Keighley, West Yorkshire, and hopes to impress his guests with a 'stylish with swimwear' dress code and hot-tub disco, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah from Wiltshire fell in love with Bulgaria 30 years ago when she was working as a teacher, and now she can't wait to buy a holiday home there. She loves the traditional town of Tryavna's authentic atmosphere and beautiful countryside, and is after a three-bedroom house for her budget of £40,000. Jonnie Irwin shows Sarah and her best friend Suzie five properties to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets hospital worker Sharon, who is searching for a holiday home in Torrevieja on Spain's Costa Blanca with a budget of £50,000

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats nine-month-old cockapoo Betsy, whose owners Sophie and Dan are concerned about a dragging front paw caused by nerve damage, to see if there are surgical options. But when Betsy develops gangrene, she has to have her paw amputated, and in order to salvage the rest of her limb, Noel must replace her amputated foot with a skeletally-anchored implant called a PerFiTS (Percutaneous Fixation to the Skeleton)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary going on the frontline with police in South Yorkshire, revealing what happens when people decide to take revenge on each other. On a Saturday night in Sheffield, police respond when a man reports that he's being attacked by his partner after she found him with a prostitute. A woman is under siege from a neighbour who claims he is sorting out neighbourhood problems by smashing cars with a hammer. And on the outskirts of Doncaster, there's a brawl outside a supermarket as three men attack someone they suspect of causing their family harm

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: Prue Leith and Dr Rupy Aujla help a couple who waste food by preparing back-up meals for their fussy children, putting their shopping bills to double the national average. They come up with a clever plan that could save £4000 a year - if the kids will eat it. Recipes include a crushed cornflake-coated chicken burger, a fresh Asian salad with the unlikely star ingredient of unwanted sandwich ham, and a repurposed leftover takeaway curry

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams aiming to clean up the country, including internet entrepreneur Martin, and his partner-in-grime Glen, who have had enough of the fly-tippers plaguing their beloved Hampshire countryside and have decided to fight back using all means at their disposal. In the North East, the Howdon Sewage Treatment plant not only cleans over 12,000 litres of waste water a second, but even manages to turn it into a gas used to heat homes across the region. But to do it, head honcho Tony has to make sure his plant remains blockage free under a constant assault from wet wipes, sanitary products, false teeth and even the occasional 20ft python

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: A dodgy scrap dealer is spotted up to no good in County Durham, but his attempt to flee the grime scene is captured in evidentially invaluable technicolour. In Surrey, a pair of water engineers head out to tackle the damage caused to the UK's sewer system by some of the 11 billion litres of effluent flushed down British U-bends every day, while Malvern's enforcement officers Dan and Jude spend their days hunting for fly-tippers and rubbish dumpers, but need to get their prevention message across in a different way

programme.title: 100 Amazing Animal Rescues
programme.desc: A selection of the bizarre, heartwarming and downright dangerous ways animals have been rescued across the world, all of it told through amateur video content found online - whether it be elephants stuck in the mud, ducks down a drain, or furry friends caught in fences

programme.title: The Man in the Iron Mask (1998)
programme.desc: The Three Musketeers set out to depose the villainous King of France and reinstate his long-imprisoned twin brother. programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following British people living it up on the Costa del Sol. Bambi shows newcomers Howard and Lynne around the caravan park and treats them to a Bollywood evening. But unaware to the couple the host has recently gone vegan, so it turns out to be a meat-free knees-up. Kim and Matt reopen their chip shop after a devastating fire which forced them to shut for a year and Harrison is down on the marina, trying to sell tickets for the Black Pearl pirate ship tour

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Expats Russell and Lindsay hope to attract punters to their convenience store with a selection of pastries, while retired nurse Susie and her husband Reg, who volunteer at a dog shelter, have their hands full with a littler of 10 puppies. Life coach Svetlana's online rants about the behaviour of expats on the Costa del Sol have gone viral. But as she prepares to record another monologue, she has a surprise up her sleeve

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Artists David and Carol, who have been left with a shortfall in their UK pension after living abroad for years, try to sell some of their work at a market in Fuengirola. Campsite dweller and DJ Noel - who has a 10-year plan to buy a bar - gears up to spin tunes at his biggest gig of the year, the National San Juan festival. Cameras also follow former Playboy bunny Michelle and her husband Tony, who have taken advantage of the Costa's roasting heat and bought an awning company

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall helps famous faces solve their dogs' behavioural problems, using his experience to find quick but long-lasting solutions. Graeme visits Essex, where actress and presenter Denise Van Outen is being given the runaround by her French bulldog Tilly. He then meets opera singer Russell Watson, whose saluki Poppy has a fear of other dogs, before helping former England striker Michael Owen, whose Staffordshire bull terrier Ronnie has attacked dogs in the past

programme.title: Psycho Pussies 2: Mad Cat Attacks!
programme.desc: Online clips and live case studies explore some bad feline behaviour, including those fearlessly ferocious types that think nothing of attacking their loyal owners. Owner Cath found herself hospitalised for four days after her savage cat Milo attacked her, while Deezel's owner Fiona has had to build an impenetrable wall to prevent the feline attacking three dogs. Elsewhere, aptly-named Sid Vicious is a feral punk rocker of a cat who eats six times a day. Will owner Janice ever be able to tame this animal with an attitude?

programme.title: Charles & Harry: Father & Son Divided
programme.desc: Royal experts look back on key moments in the lives of Prince Charles and his younger son to chart the development of their estrangement from its earliest days during the break-up of Charles's marriage to Diana, Princess of Wales. They examine moments of crisis throughout Harry's life to explore what more could his father have done for him, and ask what the pair can do to resolve their estrangement

programme.title: Diana: Queen of Hearts?
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at Diana, Princess of Wales' devotion to charity work and good causes, including the moment in 1987 when she made headlines by shaking hands with an Aids patient. But was her compassion a genuine attempt to change the world or a push to change her public image? Former private secretary Patrick Jephson, butler Paul Burrell and bodyguard Ken Wharfe are among those exploring the nature of Diana's charity work and her fragile state of mind as her marriage disintegrated

programme.title: Critical Surgery: Changing Lives
programme.desc: Every year 20 volunteer medics travel to Ethiopia with the Facing Africa charity, to perform reconstructive facial surgery on patients with little or no health care facilities. The first of two documentaries about their work focuses on 22-year-old Zinash, who has a tumour that has been growing for more than six years. It has now taken over her lower jaw and will kill her if not treated. The Facing Africa medics are her only hope - but this is the first time they have undertaken such a complex operation. Can they save Zinash?

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: The crimes of Peter Sutcliffe, one of Britain's most prolific and infamous serial killers, who dubbed the 'Yorkshire Ripper', terrorised northern England during the mid-1970s. He claimed 13 female victims, mainly prostitutes, before his capture in 1981

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Nadia, the daughter of a self-made Jordanian businessman samples life with teenage mum-of-two Natalie, who has had to scrub other people's toilets just to get by

programme.title: Dirty John
programme.desc: Debra discovers John's criminal record, but even hard evidence about his past of abuse, stalking and scamming is not enough to convince her of his true intentions

programme.desc: Expats Graham and Linda hope want to open a takeaway in Benalmadena which specialises in selling pies, but their dream has been put on hold until the council grants them permission. programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of more hotels and B&Bs around Britain, beginning with a visit to the Rosehill House Hotel in Burnley, Lancashire. Owned for the past 25 years by John and run by his daughter Emma, they have cluttered the 31-room establishment with tat and lost sight of who their customers are. Recently, bookings have taken a sharp decline and to make matters worse John's wife Jacquie - once the life and soul of the hotel - has been forced to retire due to illness, leaving Emma to run the place alone

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Braintree's waste enforcement officers crack down on dog owners who are leaving the region in a mess. Plus, Malvern waste warriors Dan and Jude hunt down bags of rotten refuse that have been washed into the local waterways

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Some sleuthing from the road crime team in North Yorkshire uncovers a kitchen where forgers are turning out Scottish £20 notes, while equally dogged investigating leads officers to a man using multiple fake number plates across his fleet of vehicles in an attempt to avoid detection of his numerous offences. Meanwhile, a classic high-speed pursuit and tactical stop on the motorway is undertaken to catch criminals who have stolen a boiler from a plumber's merchant

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In Bradford, a stolen car speeds at 100mph through the city as police officers track and pursue it, then forcibly stop the delinquent driver. In Leeds, a car grabs the attention of cops because where they expect to see a middle-aged woman driving, there is instead a teenager

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers follow a stolen BMW fitted with a tracking device as it heads for a cargo ship, while a drunk driver is pursued through Epping Forest. Documentary series profiling the work of a high-speed police interception unit in Essex

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers pursue two knife-wielding criminals driving a stolen vehicle, and the number plate on a drug dealer's car flashes up on the police radar, leading to a risky interception

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The unit is called to the heart of the Essex countryside to help rescue a farmworker pinned beneath a tractor, and the air support crew is sent out at night to hunt down a car thief in Southend. Meanwhile, the interceptors pursue a rogue driver around the back alleys of north-east London

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall trains a German shepherd anxious about her puppy, a bulldog that is always stealing food and faces a particular challenge with a picnic, and a boxer that is driving a wedge between its owners

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: A disgusted Ned and Terese confront Paul over his visit to Holden and force him to undo the deal. Nicolette is thrilled to open the new, refurbished Harold's Café

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Dean is shocked when he discovers Ziggy kicked Mackenzie out for hitting on Tane. Ari and Mia have a hard time discussing trying for another baby

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Fox family from Bradford swap houses, budgets and lives with the James family from the Home Counties. Jamie lives in a rented house with her husband Lee and three children, but with few opportunities, she can only work part-time in a local café and Lee has just qualified as a lorry driver. Jessen James has made millions in business and now trains and mentors businesspeople in sales, marketing and mindset. During the week Jessen and his family have to live off a budget just three per cent the size of their normal disposable income

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Tiana is astonished that Candace and her two kids live in a two-bed flat. But as she cooks dinner for only £6, her wealthy father hopes she comes back a changed character

programme.title: Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters (2013)
programme.desc: The fairytale brother and sister grow up to be renowned witch hunters, using advanced weaponry to battle the supernatural. Their latest case leads to an encounter with a powerful sorceress stealing children for an arcane ritual and a discovery about their own past. Fantasy adventure, with Gemma Arterton, Jeremy Renner and Famke Janssen

programme.title: Killer Psychopaths
programme.desc: Former prison governor and criminologist David Wilson examines the motivations and background of serial killer Joanna Dennehy, a Peterborough mother-of-two who was responsible for the murder of three men in the space of 10 days in March 2013. Dennehy's ex-partner John Treanor speaks candidly to the cameras, and there are also interviews with CPS prosecutor Chris McCann, crime reporter Ben Kendall and Chiara and Dino Lee, whose father Kevin was one of her victims

programme.title: The Murder of Rhys Jones: What Happened Next
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of 11-year-old Rhys Jones in Liverpool in August 2007, an innocent child caught in the crossfire of a city's gang culture. The programme tells the tragic tale through interviews with friends, police on the case, and key politicians who had to deal with the fallout from a murder that shocked the nation

programme.title: Gypsy Eviction: The Battle for Dale Farm
programme.desc: Although travellers legally owned the land of Dale Farm in Essex, they did not have planning permission to develop it and the site grew in size. Early attempts by the council to move them on were thwarted by the New Labour government, and the adoption of the Human Rights Laws resulted in a stalemate between the resident travellers and the council that lasted a decade. Through interviews with people on both sides of the eviction, this film looks back at the dramatic events of 2011

programme.title: Rich Kids, Skint Holiday
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Dasha from Worcestershire, who is accustomed to the finest things in life, joins Kate Harris and five of her eight kids on a basic camping trip to west Wales

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Two expats buy the bar they discovered on their honeymoon 21 years before, while a tattooist moonlights as both a reggae singer and a DIY visual artist. A Scottish couple bought a bargain Segway business, but making it work takes a lot of hard graft

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits Horley near Gatwick to help Quasim, the owner, improve his ailing Surrey guesthouse. With 13 other B&Bs all battling for the same trade, he is struggling to make the monthly repayments. Alex can see the potential in Quasim's business, but curbing his expensive tastes to maximise profits turns out to be far from easy

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Oxford litter-picker Gary is shocked when he discovers the biggest collection of used hypodermic needles yet in one of the city's clandestine drug dens, while Walsall assembles a crack clean-up crew to sift through a mound of flytipped rubbish for clues to who dumped it

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire's road crime team chases a gang stealing 4x4s from industrial estates every night, while West Yorkshire traffic cops pursue stolen vehicles across Bradford. In Leeds, a 19-year-old risks running across a busy road to meet his girlfriend and the resulting incident requires the full range of emergency services - including an air ambulance - to be deployed to try to save his life

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire is considered a soft touch by criminals, who using the county's rural back roads to operate under cover of darkness. In this edition, the team's elite officers hunt down and arrest burglars, drug dealers and thieves. Jamie Theakston narrates

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Car thieves steal a BMW and as they lead officers in a high speed chase across north London their attempts to escape become more and more desperate. Another driver is spotted swerving across the motorway, and the Interceptors find him to be four times over the drink driving limit. Meanwhile, a group of teenagers is pulled over for a routine stop-and-search, and found to be armed and ready for any encounter

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers pursue international car thieves as they try to leave the country in a stolen BMW, and police chase a drink-driver through Epping Forest. In Southend, burglary suspects fleeing back to London find themselves surrounded by multiple interceptor units

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The unit's hi-tech helicopter is called on to help pursue a motorcyclist riding at speeds of up to 130mph and showing no sign of slowing down for oncoming traffic or police cars. A petrol thief risks danger when trying to destroy evidence, and an officer tests the latest weapon in the fight against crime - a Subaru Impreza STi

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall treats a rescue dog that steals food and barks at the front door - but finds the training techniques for one problem do not work quite as well with the other. Elsewhere, a Jack Russell with strong reactions to traffic makes going for a walk a potentially lethal experience, and two akitas have an aggressive dislike of all other dogs, which the owners fear may turn violent

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Ned and Sheila think that by concentrating on work they will forget their feelings. Levi's romantic gesture piques Bea's guilt

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf, Irene and Marilyn share their concerns over Justin's erratic behaviour. Dean finds a drunk Mackenzie holed up at the motel, and after harsh words, they become closer

programme.title: Sinkholes: Buried Underground
programme.desc: The personal stories of people who were almost killed by these natural phenomena, which can swallow streets, rip up motorways and even drag unfortunate bystanders to their deaths

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces out on the road and in the air to discover how CCTV and technological advances are being used to fight crime. Footage includes some residents taking neighbourhood watch to the next level, a group of thugs caught wrongdoing on a housing estate and a pensioner using her walking stick to defend herself against an intruder

programme.title: Husbands from Hell
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on partners who give their wives grief, including Alan in Oxfordshire who turned the family garden into a scrapyard and just cannot stop buying cars - 120 of them at the last count. Jane from Dunfermline has ended up with a Mr Bean lookalike for a husband thanks to her partner Bill, while opera singer Nicky in Bury has to put up with her husband's obsession with cleaning the house at the crack of dawn

programme.title: My Lover My Killer
programme.desc: The case of Ian Paton, a man with a history of violence sought to start a family with his on-off girlfriend, but fatherhood was not enough to curb his anger

programme.title: Halloween H20 (1998)
programme.desc: Homicidal madman Michael Myers returns to stalk his original target, now an alcoholic teacher, 20 years after his first killing spree, and ends up terrorising a group of teenagers in a deserted school. Horror sequel, starring Jamie Lee Curtis, Janet Leigh, Josh Hartnett and Michelle Williams

programme.title: The Gypsies Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary following travellers' constant struggle to co-exist with settled communities. Carol and her family cannot understand why her community is so hated and vilified, while local man Arthur is on a mission to understand what brings the travellers into parks and public areas - and Carol's caravan is his first stop. Johnny and his family live in a chalet in Essex but his non-traveller wife Andrea has had enough of feeling ostracised and wants to move back into a normal house

programme.title: Happy Campers: The Caravan Park
programme.desc: At Tencreek holiday park in Looe, Cornwall, new Yellow Coats Charley and Andi make their debuts as park mascots Deano and Sparky, while trouble in the toilet block puts park manager Rob in the firing line from an irate customer. Over in Somerset, Holiday Resort Unity's head of catering Sharon has to turn around the park's new burger joint in record time

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall's casebook starts with a broken bond between two best friends. Cairn terrier Ted has turned on his owner Mike after having a stroke and now Mike can't even cross his front room without Ted attacking him. Can Graeme help them pave the way back to friendship? There's a little girl's best friend, who is perfect at home, but a nightmare in the neighbourhood, leaving mum Jess so stressed out with his bad behaviour that if things don't change, she wants to rehome him

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Ned and Sheila think that by concentrating on work they will forget their feelings. Levi's romantic gesture piques Bea's guilt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gibbs emerges from his coma, but is suffering from amnesia - hindering the team's attempts to unravel a terrorist plot, as he is the only person likely to possess information on the would-be perpetrator's plans. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Columbo: Identity Crisis (1975)
programme.desc: A CIA operative arranges a grisly fate for a fellow spy who found out that he was working as a double agent. The killer makes the murder look like a random mugging, but he is not as clever as he thinks and the crumpled cop soon begins to pick up on the trail of clues he has left behind. Crime drama, directed by and starring Patrick McGoohan, with Peter Falk and Leslie Nielsen

programme.title: Columbo: Old Fashioned Murder (1976)
programme.desc: A woman who has devoted her life to running her family's museum is outraged when her brother decides to sell it. Knowing of someone with criminal connections, she arranges to have her brother killed. However, just when she starts to think she has got away with it, a certain shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Joyce Van Patten, Celeste Holm and Tim O'Connor

programme.title: Columbo: Short Fuse (1972)
programme.desc: The son of a chemical company's founder is blackmailed by his uncle, who wants to force him into selling the business. The cunning heir responds by murdering his relative with a box of exploding cigars, but finds himself facing the dishevelled detective, who is convinced the resulting fatal detonation was no accident. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Roddy McDowall and Ida Lupino

programme.title: Columbo: A Stitch in Crime (1973)
programme.desc: Surgeon Barry Mayfield decides he needs get rid of his colleague to speed along a research project. When his partner requires heart surgery, Mayfield performs the operation himself and discreetly arranges some fatal complications. However, when he also murders a nurse who suspects the truth, he attracts the attention of the slovenly sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Leonard Nimoy and Will Geer

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Dangerous Match (1973)
programme.desc: An American chess champion fears he is about to be beaten by a superior player from Russia, and decides the only sure-fire way he can avoid defeat is by murdering his opponent. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Laurence Harvey and Jack Kruschen

programme.title: Columbo: By Dawn's Early Light (1974)
programme.desc: The commandant of a military academy is outraged by the chairman of the board's plans to admit female cadets, so he stages a fatal accident involving a ceremonial cannon. However, Columbo is not fooled by the deception and sets out to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Patrick McGoohan, Tom Simcox and Mark Wheeler

programme.title: Columbo: A Deadly State of Mind (1975)
programme.desc: A philandering psychiatrist, who involved his mistress in a series of hypnosis experiments, kills her husband in the heat of the moment when the pair are caught out. He persuades his distraught lover to make up a story involving two burglars, but Lt Columbo realises she is lying and goes in search of the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, George Hamilton and Lesley Warren

programme.title: Columbo: Suitable for Framing (1971)
programme.desc: A millionaire art collector is thought to have been killed as the result of a random burglary. However, Columbo is convinced the dead man's nephew committed the crime to avoid being written out of the will, and embarks on a mission to ensnare his suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ross Martin and Don Ameche

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Changes are afoot that will affect the team, who investigate a case of child neglect, and take on the child's mother and social services

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Racial tensions begin to boil over after an unarmed black man is shot by police from another precinct, and three officers are put on trial

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team is forced to return to an old crime scene, and when a case is taken to court, blackmail and deception lead to dramatic events

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: The author poses as a college professor so he can investigate the gruesome murder of a student, but he and Beckett soon discover dark secrets the university wants to keep hidden

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: A famously reclusive author turns up dead and Castle is determined to solve his hero's murder, but he and Beckett discover that truth is stranger than fiction as they dig deeper

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed
